findings.claim: PROX1 is expressed in 84% of LGE/CGE/POA-derived cortical interneurons and labels nearly all CGE-lineage cortical interneuron subtypes including 100% of VIP-expressing cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Results: PROX1 Identifies CR- NPY- RLN- and VIP-expressing Interneurons in the Adult Cortex Data so far showed that PROX1 is expressed in 84% of all LGE/CGE/POA-derived cortical interneurons ( Fig. 3C ).
findings.effect_size: 84% of LGE/CGE/POA-derived interneurons PROX1+; 100% of VIP-expressing population PROX1+
findings.evidence: Body section, Results: shown by genetic lineage tracing using Lhx6-Cre;Dlx1-Venus and Nkx5.1-Cre;R26R-YFP; quantification carried out in primary somatosensory cortex of P30 mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: PROX1 was found to be expressed in large numbers of calretinin (CR)-, neuropeptide Y (NPY)- and RLN-positive cells as well as in 100% of the VIP-expressing population ( Fig. 4A,B ).

figure_data.metric: % PROX1+ within fate-mapped CGE/LGE/POA-derived interneurons (P30 mouse S1)
figure_data.context: PROX1 IHC overlap with Lhx6-Cre;Dlx1-Venus AND Nkx5.1-Cre;R26R-YFP fate-mapped subpopulation
All VIP-expressing cortical cells co-express PROX1

findings.claim: A subset of CA1 VIP+ interneurons projects long-range axons to subiculum and is recruited preferentially during quiet wakefulness rather than during theta-run/locomotion, contrasting with classical disinhibitory VIP cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Functionally, VIP-LRP cells correspond to theta-off cells 31 , 32 as they decrease their activity during theta-run epochs associated with locomotion and exhibit high activity during quiet wakefulness.
findings.effect_size: 28 VIP+ cells analyzed in detail across 409 locomotion and 356 immobility periods
findings.evidence: Body, Results: in vivo two-photon calcium imaging of VIP+ interneurons in CA1 O/A of awake VIP-eGFP mice; combined with retrograde bead labelling from subiculum.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Among 54 CA1 VIP+ interneurons imaged, 28 VIP+ cells were located within O/A and analyzed in detail during 409 locomotion and 356 immobility periods (10–15 periods/cell pooled from two independent imaging sessions of 5 min each/3 mice; Table S4 ).
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus CA1, VIP-eGFP, in vivo 2-photon Ca2+ imaging

findings.claim: Postmitotic deletion of Prox1 in early postnatal VIP interneurons differentially impairs excitatory input dynamics onto bipolar versus multipolar VIP subtypes, and reduces Elfn1 function selectively in multipolar VIP cells, demonstrating that Prox1 acts as a postmitotic specifier of VIP subtype diversification.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Thus, in addition to activity-dependent processes that contribute to the developmental trajectory of VIP cells, genetic programs engaged by Prox1 control the final differentiation of multipolar and bipolar subtypes.
findings.effect_size: Differential effect: Prox1 cKO reduces Elfn1-dependent short-term plasticity in multipolar but not bipolar VIP cells
findings.evidence: Body, Results/Discussion: VIPCre × conditional Prox1 allele to remove Prox1 during first postnatal week; synaptic in vitro electrophysiology, pharmacology, and genetic experiments in male and female mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Further genetic, pharmacological, and electrophysiological experiments demonstrate that removing Prox1 reduces Elfn1 function in VIP multipolar but not in bipolar cells.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex (postnatal), VIPCre conditional Prox1 KO, in vitro slice electrophysiology

findings.claim: Cortical calretinin-expressing interneurons constitute about 10–30% of GABAergic interneurons; CGE-derived CR+ cells are predominantly VIP-bipolar, whereas dorsal-MGE-derived CR+ cells form SOM-Martinotti-like populations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical calretinin (CR)-expressing interneurons represent a heterogeneous subpopulation of about 10–30% of GABAergic interneurons, which altogether total ca. 12–20% of all cortical neurons.
findings.effect_size: 10–30% of GABAergic interneurons; 12–20% of all cortical neurons
findings.evidence: Body, Introduction: review synthesizing rodent neocortex literature on CGE vs dorsal-MGE origins and CR+ interneuron subtypes.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Cortical calretinin (CR) expressing interneurons represent a heterogeneous subpopulation of about 10–30% of GABAergic interneurons (Kubota et al., 1994 ; Gonchar and Burkhalter, 1997 ; Tamamaki et al., 2003 ).

findings.claim: Conditional removal of all AMPAR subunits (GluA1/2/3 KO) in CGE-derived interneurons increases their early postnatal numbers but causes later apoptosis to converge with wild-type, reduces feedforward and feedback inhibition onto pyramidal neurons, and produces behavioural abnormalities — establishing that AMPAR-mediated glutamatergic drive is required for postnatal maturation of CGE-derived interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Moreover, in GluA1-3 KOs, the number of interneurons invading the hippocampus increased in the early postnatal period but converged with WT numbers later due to increased apoptosis.
findings.effect_size: GluA1-3 KO almost completely eliminates sEPSCs in CGE interneurons; reduces feedforward and feedback inhibition onto pyramidal neurons
findings.evidence: Body, Results/Discussion: conditional knockout of GluA1&2&3 in CGE-derived interneurons; sEPSC recordings, anatomical density, behaviour.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Removal of GluA1&2&3 subunits (GluA1-3 KO) in CGE-derived interneurons, almost completely eliminated sEPSCs without further reducing synapse density, but increased dendritic branching.
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus, conditional GluA KO in CGE-derived interneurons

findings.claim: In tuberous sclerosis complex (TSC) patient cortex, somatostatin-expressing (SST+) MGE-derived interneurons display an immature transcriptional signature including an abnormal NKCC1/KCC2 ratio, suggesting an incomplete excitatory-to-inhibitory GABAergic developmental switch — a CGE-vs-MGE lineage-selective vulnerability with implications for postnatal interneuron maturation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The disrupted maturation of SST+ interneurons may undergo an incomplete switch from excitatory to inhibitory GABAergic signaling during development, resulting in reduced inhibitory properties.
findings.effect_size: MGE-derived SST+ subpopulation shows abnormal NKCC1/KCC2 ratio and downregulated GABA-A receptor subunits relative to controls
findings.evidence: Body, Results: single-nuclei RNA-seq in TSC patient cortical material, with cell-type-specific marker analysis of MGE- vs CGE-derived interneurons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Notably, this study reveals markers of immaturity specifically in SST+ interneurons, including an abnormal NKCC1/KCC2 ratio, indicating an imbalance in chloride homeostasis crucial for the postsynaptic consequences of GABAergic signaling as well as the downregulation of GABA A receptor subunits.

findings.claim: Cell-autonomous reduction of activity in CGE-derived cortical interneurons by E15.5 in utero Kir2.1 electroporation produces subtype-specific developmental alterations: reelin+ and CR+ cells acquire abnormal laminar positioning and altered EPSC kinetics, while intrinsic firing matures more slowly across all CGE subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Indeed, reducing neuronal excitability within this population, by expressing the inward rectifying potassium channel Kir2.1 causes Re+ and Cr+ subtypes to acquire abnormal laminar positioning.
findings.effect_size: Kir2.1 reduced AP discharge from 93±10 Hz to 44±8 Hz; widened AP half-width from 1.3±0.15 ms to 3±0.32 ms
findings.evidence: Body, Results: in utero electroporation of Kir2.1 at E15.5 to silence CGE-lineage interneurons, followed by patch-clamp characterization of intrinsic properties and sEPSC/sIPSC kinetics in reelin+, CR+, and VIP+ subgroups.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: This difference likely underlies the reduced maximum discharge frequency of APs observed in Kir2.1-expressing interneurons compared to controls (Kir2.1: 44 ± 7.89 Hz, n = 9 vs. controls: 93 ± 10.06 Hz, n = 6; p = 0.0004) upon a 500 ms-long square current pulse.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, in utero electroporation E15.5, slice patch-clamp

figure_data.context: CGE-derived interneurons P12–P15 cortex; Kir2.1 expression reduces excitability
Same CGE cells; Kir2.1 broadens action potential

findings.claim: During the critical period for binocular plasticity in mouse V1, basal-forebrain acetylcholine directly excites SST interneurons, enabling compartmentalized dendritic spiking; this cholinergic responsiveness is lost in adulthood, and suppressing SST cells during the critical period prevents normal development of binocular receptive fields.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Conversely, suppressing SST cell activity during the critical period prevents the normal development of binocular receptive fields by impairing the maturation of ipsilateral eye inputs.
findings.effect_size: Direct cholinergic response present at P28 but absent at P56 in SST cells
findings.evidence: Body, Results: in vivo two-photon imaging of L2/3 pyramidal dendrites in V1, slice recordings of SST and VIP interneurons, optogenetic SST suppression at P28 vs P56.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Notably, this direct cholinergic response was not present at P56, despite unchanged intrinsic properties ( Fig. 1e–f , Extended Data Fig. 2 ).
findings.study_system: mouse primary visual cortex, P28 vs P56, acute slice and in vivo imaging

findings.claim: Nkx6-2-expressing ventral telencephalic progenitors generate a heterogeneous mix of cortical interneuron subtypes that span both MGE- and CGE-associated phenotypes; Nkx6-2 loss-of-function produces only a small, partially penetrant reduction in delayed non-fast-spiking SST/calretinin double-positive cortical interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Analysis of these populations in Nkx6-2 null mice suggests that there is a small and partially penetrant loss of delayed non-fast spiking somatostatin/calretinin double positive cortical interneurons in the absence of Nkx6-2 gene function.
findings.effect_size: Small, partially penetrant reduction in SST/CR double-positive interneurons
findings.evidence: Body, Results: genetic inducible fate mapping (CreER) of Nkx6-2 lineage combined with in vivo loss-of-function analysis and patch-clamp electrophysiology in juvenile mouse cortex.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, Nkx6-2 fate mapping and null mutant

findings.claim: Optogenetic activation of VIP+ interneurons in mouse auditory cortex fails to improve stimulus encoding and instead reduces encoding efficiency; in contrast to visual cortex, where VIP-driven disinhibition is necessary and sufficient for movement-related enhancement, auditory cortex VIP activation does not gate sensory processing in the same way — revealing area-specific consequences of the canonical VIP disinhibitory motif.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Whereas enhanced stimulus processing in visual cortex is thought to depend on VIP + interneuron activation, optogenetic activation of VIP + interneurons in auditory cortex failed to improve stimulus processing.
findings.effect_size: VIP+ activation increased stimulus-evoked spike rates but did not increase stimulus–response mutual information, reducing encoding efficiency
findings.evidence: Body, Results/Discussion: optogenetic activation of VIP+ interneurons on half of trials in awake head-fixed mice during sound presentation in auditory cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP interneuron activation tended to increase stimulus-evoked spike rates but not stimulus–response mutual information, thus reducing encoding efficiency.
findings.study_system: mouse auditory cortex, VIP-Cre × ChR2, awake head-fixed

findings.claim: Cortical 5-HT3A receptor-expressing interneurons are predominantly generated in the caudal ganglionic eminence; in utero CGE grafts of E14 Htr3a-GFP+ cells reproduce the supragranular laminar distribution and neurochemical profile (VIP, NPY, reelin, calretinin) characteristic of CGE-lineage interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Various techniques including homochronic in utero grafts performed at the peak of genesis of cortical 5-HT 3A + interneurons revealed that these neurons are mainly generated in the CGE.
findings.effect_size: In Htr3a-GFP+ NPY cluster: 52% reelin+; in VIP cluster: 23% reelin+
findings.evidence: Body, Results: Tg(Htr3a-GFP) mouse line, in utero grafts of CGE vs AEP/POA explants, immunohistochemistry, and patch-clamp recordings.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Interestingly, Reelin was expressed in 52% of cells in the NPY-cluster, whereas only 23% of cells of the VIP-cluster contained this marker.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, Tg(Htr3a-GFP), in utero CGE grafts

figure_data.context: 5HT3aR-GFP+ NPY-cluster CGE-derived interneurons in mouse cortex
5HT3aR-GFP+ VIP-cluster CGE-derived interneurons in mouse cortex

findings.claim: In CGE-derived reelin+ and calretinin+ (but not VIP+) cortical interneurons, neuronal activity is essential before postnatal day 3 for correct migration; after P3, glutamate-mediated activity controls axon and dendrite development. The Dlx1 target gene Elmo1 is selectively expressed in Re+/Cr+ subtypes and mediates this activity-dependent migration.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we demonstrate that for CGE-derived Re + and Cr + (but not VIP + ) interneurons 12 - 13 , activity is essential before postnatal day 3 (P3) for correct migration, and that after P3, glutamate-mediated activity controls the development of their axons and dendrites.
findings.effect_size: ELMO1 is expressed by Re+ and Cr+, but not VIP+ subtypes; Kir2.1 downregulates ELMO1
findings.evidence: Body, Results: in utero Dlx5/6-eGFP electroporation at E15.5; Kir2.1 cell-autonomous silencing; subdural kynurenic acid blockade P0–P3; Elmo1 dominant-negative co-electroporation; analysis at P8-P9 and P21.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We found that ELMO1 is expressed by Re + and Cr + , but not VIP + subtypes and is downregulated upon Kir2.1 expression ( Fig. 5b-c ).
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, in utero electroporation E15.5, P0–P21

findings.claim: CGE-derived cortical interneurons begin generation at E12.5 and peak at E16.5 (about 3 days later than MGE), do not follow inside-out lamination but preferentially (75%) occupy superficial cortical layers regardless of birthdate, and constitute ~30% of all cortical interneurons across at least nine distinct subtypes; most are reelin- or VIP-positive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Moreover, unlike either pyramidal cells or MGE-derived cortical interneurons, CGE-derived interneurons do not integrate into the cortex in an inside-out manner but preferentially (75%) occupy superficial cortical layers independent of birthdate.
findings.effect_size: ~30% of all cortical interneurons; 75% in superficial layers; ≥9 distinct subtypes
findings.evidence: Abstract: inducible CreER genetic fate mapping in mouse combined with electrophysiological characterization across embryonic timepoints E12.5–E16.5.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In contrast to previous estimates, CGE-derived interneurons are both considerably greater in number (∼30% of all cortical interneurons) and diversity (comprised by at least nine distinct subtypes).
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, inducible Mash1/Olig2-CreER fate mapping

figure_data.metric: % of all cortical interneurons derived from CGE
figure_data.context: Mash1-CreER fate mapping in mouse cortex

figure_data.metric: % of CGE-derived interneurons in superficial layers (II/III)
figure_data.context: Mouse cortex postnatal

findings.claim: Hippocampal interneurons are produced in two distinct neurogenic waves — MGE between E9–E12 and CGE between E12–E16 — and invade the hippocampus by E14; in the mature hippocampus, CGE-derived interneurons preferentially populate strata lacunosum moleculare and deep radiatum (CCK+, calretinin+, VIP+, reelin+ subtypes), while MGE-derived populations dominate strata pyramidale and oriens (parvalbumin+, somatostatin+).
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that hippocampal interneurons are produced in two neurogenic waves between E9-E12 and E12-E16 from MGE and CGE, respectively, and invade the hippocampus by E14.
findings.effect_size: MGE wave E9–E12; CGE wave E12–E16; invasion by E14
findings.evidence: Abstract: combined molecular, anatomical, and electrophysiological characterization in MGE- and CGE-specific transgenic and inducible mouse lines.
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus, transgenic and inducible MGE/CGE reporter mice

findings.claim: The transcription factor Prox1 is selectively maintained in postmitotic CGE-derived cortical interneuron precursors; loss of Prox1 impairs their integration into superficial cortical layers and differentially regulates the postnatal maturation of RELN+, calbindin2/VIP+, and VIP+ subtypes, working in tandem with activity-dependent extrinsic pathways.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we report that in mice expression of the transcription factor Prox1 is selectively maintained in postmitotic CGE-derived cortical interneuron precursors and that loss of Prox1 impairs the integration of these cells into superficial layers.
findings.effect_size: Prox1 first identified transcription factor required for CGE-derived interneuron acquisition of subtype-specific properties; differential subtype effects on RELN+, Calb2/VIP+, VIP+
findings.evidence: Abstract: conditional Prox1 deletion in CGE-derived interneurons of mouse cortex; combined with morphological, immunohistochemical and electrophysiological characterization across postnatal stages.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Moreover, Prox1 differentially regulates the postnatal maturation of each specific subtype originating from the CGE (RELN, Calb2/VIP, and VIP).
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, conditional Prox1 KO in CGE lineage

findings.claim: CGE-derived cortical interneurons reach the neocortex, hippocampus and amygdala via three distinct caudo-rostral migratory streams — the lateral (LMS), medial (MMS), and previously known caudal (CMS) — that emerge in a precise temporal sequence and express distinct combinations of SP8, PROX1, COUP-TFI and COUP-TFII; conditional inactivation of COUP-TFI perturbs LMS/MMS and disrupts SP8/COUP-TFII expression, producing laminar-specific deficits of distinct interneuron subtypes in adult neocortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we unravel the existence of two novel CGE caudo-rostral migratory streams, one located laterally (LMS) and the other one more medially (MMS), that, together with the well-known caudal migratory stream (CMS), contribute to populate the neocortex, hippocampus and amygdala.
findings.effect_size: Three distinct migratory streams; loss of COUP-TFI alters SP8/COUP-TFII expression and laminar interneuron distribution
findings.evidence: Abstract: developmental analysis of mouse forebrain combined with conditional COUP-TFI inactivation and quantification of laminar interneuron distribution.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: As a consequence, adult mutant neocortices have laminar-specific alterations of distinct cortical interneuron subtypes.

findings.claim: Cortical GABAergic interneurons originate from distinct subpallial regions, with parvalbumin- and somatostatin-expressing subtypes deriving primarily from the medial ganglionic eminence (MGE) and reelin/calretinin/VIP-expressing subtypes deriving from the caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE), establishing the origin–subtype mapping that defines the MGE/CGE lineage dichotomy.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To test the hypothesis that distinct interneuron subtypes are derived from distinct telencephalic locations, we performed homochronic transplantations of medial and caudal ganglionic eminence (MGE and CGE) cells in vivo into mouse embryos.
findings.evidence: Abstract: homochronic in vivo transplantations of MGE vs CGE cells from donor embryos into host mouse cortex.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, MGE/CGE in utero transplantation

findings.claim: The embryonic preoptic area (POA) is a third subpallial source of cortical GABAergic interneurons distinct from MGE and CGE, generating a population of mainly reelin-expressing interneurons that contribute to the heterogeneity of the cortical interneuron repertoire.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Until now, interneurons have been demonstrated to derive exclusively from two subpallial structures: the medial and caudal ganglionic eminences.
findings.evidence: Abstract: combined fate mapping, in utero transplantation, and immunohistochemistry of POA precursors in mouse cortex.

findings.claim: GAD65-GFP transgenic mice label a CGE-enriched subpopulation of cortical interneurons that follow a tangential migratory route through the lower intermediate zone from E14–E15 and preferentially populate superficial cortical layers, consistent with the late-born CGE-derived interneuron program.
findings.claim_source_sentence: From E14 to E15, the highest density of GAD65-GFP cells was seen in the lower intermediate zone; however, after E15, the highest density of these cells was found in the cortical plate.
findings.evidence: Abstract: organotypic cultures, birth-dating, and immunohistochemistry on GAD65-GFP transgenic mice.

findings.claim: In neonatal mouse somatosensory cortex (P7), functional cell assemblies of interneurons and pyramidal cells emerge spatially segregated; reducing GABA release or synaptic inputs onto pyramidal cells degrades this functional topography and increases interneuron survival, indicating that GABAergic restriction of early network dynamics actively regulates interneuron developmental death.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Both reduction of GABA release and synaptic inputs onto pyramidal cells erode the emergence of functional topography, leading to increased interneuron survival.
findings.effect_size: Reduced GABA release leads to increased interneuron survival in developing somatosensory cortex
findings.evidence: Abstract: longitudinal in vivo calcium imaging in unanesthetized mouse pups, P7; pharmacological/genetic manipulations of GABA release and pyramidal-cell synaptic input.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Both reduction of GABA release and synaptic inputs onto pyramidal cells erode the emergence of functional topography, leading to increased interneuron survival.
findings.study_system: mouse somatosensory cortex, P7, in vivo 2-photon calcium imaging

findings.claim: Hippocampal nitric-oxide-synthase-expressing neurogliaform (NGC) and Ivy cells share a common embryonic origin in the caudal/medial ganglionic eminence and form the largest single nNOS+ hippocampal interneuron cohort, providing a developmental rationale for their convergent neurochemical and physiological identity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Although interneurons expressing the neuronal isoform of nitric oxide synthase (nNOS) constitute the largest hippocampal interneuron cohort their origin and specification remain unknown.
findings.evidence: Abstract: combined inducible fate mapping, immunohistochemistry, and electrophysiological characterization in mouse hippocampus.

findings.claim: Conditional Satb2 knockout in mouse projection neurons reprograms intratelencephalic-type pyramidal neurons toward a pyramidal-tract identity and selectively disrupts the lamination and circuit integration of CGE-derived (but not MGE-derived) interneurons, providing causal evidence that excitatory subtype identity instructs CGE-lineage interneuron development in a lineage-specific manner.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Loss of IT-type PNs selectively disrupted the lamination and circuit integration of INs derived from the caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE).
findings.evidence: Abstract: conditional Satb2 cKO in mouse cortex; analysis of CGE- vs MGE-lineage interneuron lamination, electrophysiology, and connectivity.

findings.claim: Mouse cortical interneuron precursors born at E12.5 from MGE versus CGE simultaneously initiate tangential migration and complete entry into the cortical plate by P1, but subsequently segregate into deep (MGE) versus superficial (CGE) layers; the onset of radial sorting coincides with upregulation of the K/Cl cotransporter KCC2.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Surprisingly, while these 2 interneuron populations show a comparable layer distribution at P1, they subsequently segregate into distinct cortical layers.
findings.effect_size: Initiation of radial sorting within each lineage coincides with upregulation of KCC2
findings.evidence: Abstract: inducible CreER fate mapping of MGE vs CGE precursors at E12.5; quantification of laminar distribution from P1 through adulthood.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In addition, the initiation of the radial sorting within each lineage coincided well with the upregulation of the potassium/chloride cotransporter KCC2.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, inducible CreER fate mapping

findings.claim: CGE-derived interneurons transplanted into neonatal mouse visual cortex disperse, acquire host-like laminar position, marker expression, intrinsic properties and visual response properties — but, unlike MGE transplants, they do not reactivate ocular dominance plasticity; the residual ODP induction observed with CGE transplants is mediated by a small contaminating fraction of MGE-lineage cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Although transplants from the anatomical CGE do induce ODP, we found that this plasticity reactivation is mediated by a small fraction of MGE-derived cells contained in the transplant.
findings.effect_size: ODP reactivation is induced by MGE-lineage cells; CGE-lineage transplants do not reactivate ODP
findings.evidence: Abstract: heterochronic transplantation of CGE precursors into neonatal mouse V1; comparison to MGE transplants; characterization by marker expression, intrinsic and sensory physiology.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These findings demonstrate that transplanted CGE cells can successfully engraft into the postnatal mouse brain and confirm the unique role of MGE lineage neurons in the induction of ODP.
findings.study_system: mouse visual cortex, heterochronic CGE transplantation, neonatal recipients

findings.claim: In second-trimester human ganglionic eminences, the subventricular zone (SVZ) is massively expanded relative to rodents and contains stem cells without radial-fiber/oriented organization; CGE-derived progenitors produce a heterogeneous interneuron population whose proliferation and migration patterns differ from MGE, suggesting human-specific aspects of CGE-lineage development.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We characterized the developing human ganglionic eminences and found that the subventricular zone (SVZ) expanded massively during the early second trimester, becoming densely populated with neural stem cells and intermediate progenitor cells.
findings.evidence: Abstract: histological and marker-based characterization of second-trimester human ganglionic eminences, focusing on SVZ stem-cell organization in MGE/CGE.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In contrast with the cortex, most stem cells in the ganglionic eminence SVZ did not maintain radial fibers or orientation.

findings.claim: Individual human cortical progenitors retrovirally barcoded in fetal cortex generate clones containing both excitatory pyramidal neurons and inhibitory interneurons, including subsets molecularly resembling CGE-lineage cells — challenging the rodent paradigm that all cortical interneurons originate exclusively from subpallial ganglionic eminences.
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, the developmental potential of human cortical progenitors has not been thoroughly explored.
findings.evidence: Abstract: retroviral barcoding of human cortical progenitors followed by clonal lineage tracing in fetal cortical samples.
findings.study_system: human fetal cortex, retroviral lineage tracing

findings.claim: The human amygdala paralaminar nuclei (PL) develop adjacent to the caudal ganglionic eminence and contain a population of immature DCX+PSA-NCAM+ neurons that mostly express excitatory markers; many transition to mature TBR1+VGLUT2+ neurons during adolescence, while local progenitor proliferation sharply decreases by infancy — establishing a protracted, CGE-adjacent developmental program in human amygdala.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The PL develops next to the caudal ganglionic eminence, which generates inhibitory interneurons, yet most PL neurons express excitatory markers.
findings.evidence: Abstract: histological and snRNA-seq characterization of human PL from embryonic stages through adulthood (4–15 years and beyond).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In children, most PL cells are immature (DCX+PSA-NCAM+), and during adolescence many transition into mature (TBR1+VGLUT2+) neurons.

findings.claim: In Lhx6−/− mice, some MGE cells aberrantly acquire a CGE-like identity; in vivo complementation shows Lhx6-regulated targets Arx and CXCR7 rescue separable aspects of Lhx6 function — Arx restores SST/PV specification while CXCR7 restores migration/laminar positioning, explaining the molecular basis of MGE versus CGE lineage divergence.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we demonstrate that some Lhx6⁻/⁻ MGE cells acquire a CGE-like fate.
findings.evidence: Abstract: in vivo MGE complementation/transplantation assay, Lhx6 KO mice with Arx and CXCR7 rescue.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, Lhx6 KO with Arx/CXCR7 rescue

findings.claim: In developing human and monkey telencephalon, transcription factor expression of Sox6, COUP-TFII and Sp8 delineates the same three main subpallial progenitor domains (MGE, LGE, CGE) found in rodents, and tangentially migrating interneurons retain those marker codes — supporting a conserved subcortical origin of primate neocortical interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that the expression patterns of several key transcription factors in the developing primate telencephalon are very similar to those in rodents, delineating the three main subcortical progenitor domains (the medial, lateral and caudal ganglionic eminences) and the interneurons tangentially migrating from them.
findings.evidence: Abstract: immunohistochemical mapping of Sox6, COUP-TFII, Sp8 in developing human and monkey telencephalon plus cell-migration evidence.

findings.claim: In mouse primary visual cortex, perisomatic GABAergic synapse formation and maturation by basket cell interneurons proceeds through a stereotyped postnatal time course but is profoundly accelerated or delayed by altered visual experience, demonstrating that sensory experience drives the late-postnatal maturation of cortical inhibition that defines critical-period onset.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the primary visual cortex, for example, it is unknown whether, and to what extent, the formation and maturation of perisomatic synapses are intrinsic to cortical circuits or are regulated by sensory experience.
findings.evidence: Abstract: GAD67-GFP BAC transgenic mice with perisomatic synapse marker labeling; dark rearing manipulations across postnatal development.
findings.study_system: mouse V1, GAD67-GFP BAC transgenic, postnatal time course

findings.claim: The zinc finger transcription factor Sp8 is expressed in neurogenic regions that produce olfactory bulb interneurons at embryonic and postnatal stages and is maintained in calretinin+ and GABAergic/non-dopaminergic glomerular layer interneurons; conditional inactivation of Sp8 reduces these subtypes, identifying Sp8 as a key transcriptional determinant of CGE-related interneuron diversity in the dorsal LGE/CGE.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Conditional inactivation of Sp8 in the embryonic ventral telencephalon reveals a requirement for the normal generation of these interneuron subtypes.
findings.evidence: Abstract: Sp8 conditional knockout in mouse embryonic ventral telencephalon, with characterization of olfactory bulb interneuron subtype composition.

findings.claim: In mouse prefrontal cortex, VIP interneuron activity increases in the open arms of the elevated plus maze and is required for hippocampal-prefrontal information flow that drives open-arm avoidance; chemogenetic inhibition of VIP interneurons disrupts network representations of open arms and reduces avoidance, showing that VIP-driven disinhibition translates single-cell circuit motifs into anxiety-related behavior.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP interneuron activity increases in the open arms and disinhibits prefrontal responses to hippocampal inputs, which are known to transmit signals related to open arm avoidance.
findings.evidence: Abstract: chemogenetic inhibition of VIP+ interneurons during EPM behavior; population-level recordings of hippocampal-prefrontal inputs.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Indeed, inhibiting VIP interneurons disrupts network-level representations of the open arms and decreases open arm avoidance specifically when hippocampal-prefrontal inputs are simultaneously perturbed.

findings.claim: Mosaic elimination of SHH effector Smo in MGE progenitors causes upregulation of GSX2 and conversion of a subset of MGE cells to a CGE-like, bipolar calretinin-expressing fate, demonstrating that SHH signaling normally restricts MGE progenitors away from a CGE-lineage program and that lineage identity is plastic during the neurogenic period.
findings.claim_source_sentence: SHH signaling prevents the upregulation of GSX2 and conversion of some MGE progenitors to a caudal ganglionic eminence-like, bipolar calretinin-expressing cell fate that is preferentially destined to occupy superficial cortical layers.
findings.evidence: Abstract: conditional Smo deletion in MGE progenitors followed by lineage tracing to cortex.

findings.claim: The chemokine Cxcl12 attracts cortical interneurons during tangential migration in subpial and intermediate-zone streams, while loss of responsiveness to Cxcl12 triggers exit from the streams; mice with Cxcr4 mutations show disorganized streams and premature radial entry into cortical plate, identifying Cxcl12/Cxcr4 signaling as a key timer of migratory stream maintenance for both MGE- and CGE-derived interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Mice with mutations in Cxcr4 have disorganized migratory streams and deletion of Cxcr4 after the streams have formed precipitates premature entry into the developing cortical plate.
findings.evidence: Abstract: conditional Cxcr4 KO and migration assays in mouse embryonic cortex.

findings.claim: In utero electroporation of mouse LGE/CGE produces two laminar-fate populations of CGE-derived neocortical interneurons — one preferentially populating layer I (reelin+/NPY+) and the other multilaminar layers — with distinct neurochemical and electrophysiological identities, demonstrating that birthdate and embryonic location within LGE/CGE causally specify the laminar fate of CGE-lineage cortical interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we performed in utero electroporation on mouse LGE/CGE and found two groups of animals, one with preferential labeling of layer I and the other with preferential labeling of other layers.
findings.evidence: Abstract: in utero electroporation of LGE/CGE in mouse embryos, immunohistochemistry, and electrophysiological characterization at postnatal stages.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex, LGE/CGE in utero electroporation

findings.claim: In the mouse neocortex, the 5HT3aR-EGFP transgenic line labels nearly all neocortical interneurons that derive from the CGE — comprising the third major non-overlapping cohort of cortical interneurons distinct from MGE-derived parvalbumin+ and somatostatin+ groups; together with PV+ and SST+ they account for nearly 100% of GABAergic neocortical neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The diversity of cortical interneurons is determined during development and primarily depends on their embryonic origins either from the medial (MGE) or the caudal (CGE) ganglionic eminences.
findings.effect_size: PV+, SST+, and 5HT3aR+ groups together account for nearly 100% of neocortical GABAergic interneurons
findings.evidence: Abstract: 5HT3aR-EGFP BAC transgenic mouse line; multiparameter cluster analysis of neocortical interneurons.

findings.claim: Genetic fate mapping in mouse shows that ventral pallium progenitors contribute projection neurons to the lateral and basolateral amygdala but not the central amygdala (CA), and CA derives partly from ventral lateral ganglionic eminence progenitors; intercalated cells (ITCs) of the amygdala originate from a distinct subpallial source — establishing that the amygdalar fear circuit's interneuron composition shares developmental origins with cortical CGE-lineage cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our data indicate that progenitors in the ventral pallium (VP) contribute projection neurons to the LA and BLA but not the CA.
findings.evidence: Abstract: genetic-based fate mapping with reporter alleles in mouse embryos; quantification of projection neuron and interneuron contributions to amygdalar nuclei.

findings.claim: Longitudinal calcium imaging in unanesthetized mouse pups shows that layer I CGE-derived interneurons (co-expressing 5HT3aR and reelin) display the highest degree of spontaneous synchronous calcium activity among neonatal cortical neurons, and disrupting their synchrony impairs sensory map refinement — implicating CGE-lineage L1 interneurons in early sensory circuit assembly.
findings.claim_source_sentence: By using longitudinal calcium imaging in unanesthetized mouse pups, we show that layer I (LI) interneurons, delineated by co-expression of the 5HT3a serotonin receptor (5HT3aR) and reelin (Re), display spontaneous calcium transients with the highest degree of synchrony among cortical neurons during neonatal development.
findings.evidence: Abstract: in vivo two-photon calcium imaging in unanesthetized mouse pups, with chemogenetic perturbation of CGE-derived L1 interneuron synchrony.
findings.study_system: mouse somatosensory cortex, P5–P14, in vivo Ca2+ imaging

findings.claim: Dlx1 and Dlx2 homeobox transcription factors restrain neurite outgrowth in subpallial-derived immature interneurons during tangential migration to neocortex; in Dlx1/2 double-mutant mice, neurite length is increased and cells fail to migrate, identifying Dlx1/2 as molecular gatekeepers that couple migratory competence to delayed neurite differentiation in both MGE- and CGE-lineage interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In Dlx1-/-;Dlx2-/- mutants, neurite length is increased and cells fail to migrate.
findings.evidence: Abstract: Dlx1/2 double-mutant mouse embryos with tangential migration assays and morphological analysis.

findings.claim: In the medial ganglionic eminence, NKX2.1 specifies cortical interneuron fate by directly activating Lhx6 expression; rescue experiments in Nkx2.1-/- slice cultures show that re-introduction of Nkx2.1 restores Lhx6 expression and PV/SST-fated interneuron specification — establishing the NKX2.1 → Lhx6 axis that distinguishes MGE-lineage from CGE-lineage interneuron identity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that electroporation of Nkx2.1 cDNA into the ventral telencephalon of slice cultures from Nkx2.1-/- mouse embryos, followed by transplantation, rescues the differentiation of Lhx6-expressing interneurons.
findings.evidence: Abstract: Nkx2.1 cDNA rescue in Nkx2.1-/- slice cultures and ChIP/in vivo expression analyses.

findings.claim: Combined Dlx5/Dlx6 deletion in mice reduces and disorders tangential migration of Lhx6+/Mafb+ interneurons to the cortex with reduced CXCR4 expression and selectively depletes parvalbumin+ neocortical interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Simultaneous deletion of Dlx5 and 6 results in exencephaly of the anterior brain; despite this defect, prenatal basal ganglia differentiation appeared largely intact, while tangential migration of Lhx6(+) and Mafb(+) interneurons to the cortex was reduced and disordered.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Genetic loss-of-function in mouse with histological, transplantation, and electrophysiological readouts; Dlx5/6 selectively required for parvalbumin+ basket cells, contrasting Dlx1's role in dendrite-targeting (calretinin/SST/NPY) interneurons.

findings.claim: Lhx6, induced in the MGE and maintained in PV+ and SST+ interneurons, is required for specification of these subtypes in neocortex and hippocampus and for normal tangential and radial migration of GABAergic interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we show that Lhx6, a gene induced in the medial ganglionic eminence and maintained in parvalbumin- and somatostatin-positive interneurons, is required for the specification of these neuronal subtypes in the neocortex and the hippocampus.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Mouse Lhx6 loss-of-function genetics with marker analysis and migration assays.

findings.claim: Sox6 is expressed continuously in MGE-derived cortical interneurons from postmitotic progenitor stages into adulthood and acts downstream of Lhx6 to control PV/SST positioning and maturation; its loss preserves immature fast-spiking properties of basket cells and produces severe epileptic encephalopathy.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We identify Sox6 as a protein expressed continuously within MGE-derived cortical interneurons from postmitotic progenitor stages into adulthood.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Mouse genetics, immunohistochemistry, and patch-clamp electrophysiology of basket cells in Sox6 conditional knockouts.

findings.claim: Nucleokinesis in tangentially migrating MGE interneurons proceeds in two alternating phases: forward translocation of the centrosome–Golgi swelling up to ~30 µm into the leading neurite, followed by myosin-II-dependent nuclear translocation toward the displaced organelles.
findings.claim_source_sentence: First, cytoplasmic organelles migrate forward, and second, the nucleus translocates toward these organelles.
findings.effect_size: centrosome moves up to 30 µm ahead of the nucleus in the leading neurite
findings.evidence: Time-lapse imaging of MGE explants combined with pharmacological perturbation (blebbistatin) of nonmuscle myosin II.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: the centrosome and the Golgi apparatus separates from the perinuclear compartment and moves rostrally into the leading neurite, up to 30 mum from the waiting nucleus.

findings.claim: Tangential migration of MGE-derived cortical interneurons is stimulated by BDNF/NT4 acting via TrkB and PI3-kinase; TrkB-null mice show fewer calbindin+ neurons migrating in embryonic cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Tangential migration is strongly stimulated by BDNF and NT4 and attenuated by the Trk-family inhibitor, K252a, suggesting that migration is regulated by TrkB signaling.
findings.effect_size: qualitative significant decrease in TrkB-null
findings.evidence: In vitro migration assay with time-lapse imaging, pharmacological inhibitors, and TrkB-null mouse genetics.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: TrkB-null mice show a significant decrease in the number of calbindin-positive neurons migrating tangentially in the embryonic cortex.
findings.study_system: mouse MGE in vitro and TrkB knockout in vivo

findings.claim: Lhx6 promotes interneuron migration through receptors ErbB4, CXCR4 and CXCR7 and through transcription factors Arx, bMaf, Cux2 and NPAS1; Lhx6 mutants show defective populating of marginal zone and superficial neocortex and absent PV+/SST+ but preserved NPY+ interneurons (likely Lhx6-;Dlx+ derived).
findings.claim_source_sentence: We provide evidence that Lhx6 mediates these effects through promoting expression of receptors that regulate interneuron migration (ErbB4, CXCR4, and CXCR7), and through promoting the expression of transcription factors either known (Arx) or implicated (bMaf, Cux2, and NPAS1) in controlling interneuron development.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Lhx6(PLAP/PLAP) loss-of-function mouse with marker analysis across neocortex, hippocampus, and striatum.

findings.claim: Cortical GABAergic interneuron diversity emerges from cell-intrinsic genetic programs in subpallial progenitors that unfold over a protracted period and are subsequently modulated by activity-dependent, non-cell-autonomous mechanisms during circuit integration.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Interneuron diversity seems to emerge through the implementation of cell-intrinsic genetic programs in progenitor cells, which unfold over a protracted period of time until interneurons acquire mature characteristics.
findings.effect_size: up to ~50 cortical interneuron types proposed
findings.evidence: Synthesis review covering subpallial origin, transcriptional specification, migration, and activity-dependent integration of cortical interneurons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Current estimates suggest that up to 50 different types of GABAergic neurons may populate the cerebral cortex, all derived from progenitor cells in the subpallium, the ventral aspect of the embryonic telencephalon.
findings.study_system: review of mouse cortical interneuron development

findings.claim: A daily-resolution single-cell atlas of mouse neocortex from embryonic to early postnatal ages reconstructs lineage trajectories and gene-regulatory programs that drive bifurcation decisions across all cortical cell classes including interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We computationally reconstruct developmental trajectories across the diversity of cortical cell classes, and infer their spatial organization and the gene regulatory programs that accompany their lineage bifurcation decisions and differentiation trajectories.
findings.effect_size: qualitative whole-cortex atlas
findings.evidence: Single-cell RNA-seq + scATAC-seq + spatial transcriptomics time course; mouse genetics validation.

findings.claim: Cross-species single-nucleus transcriptomics shows robust adult neurogenesis signatures in mouse, pig, and macaque hippocampus but not in adult human; doublecortin (DCX) is detected in diverse human neurons that do not constitute a distinct immature-neuron population.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Integrated cross-species analysis revealed robust transcriptomic and histologic signatures of neurogenesis in the adult mouse, pig, and macaque but not humans.
findings.effect_size: qualitative; species-level binary
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq across five hippocampal–entorhinal subregions in human, macaque, and pig with histology validation.
findings.study_system: adult human, macaque, pig hippocampus and entorhinal cortex

findings.claim: Adult primate dorsolateral PFC has largely conserved cell subtypes, but human-specific somatostatin–to–tyrosine-hydroxylase switching occurs in some interneurons, indicating species-divergent inhibitory neuron molecular programs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The latter are exemplified by human-specific switching between expression of the neuropeptide somatostatin and tyrosine hydroxylase, the rate-limiting enzyme in dopamine production in certain interneurons.
findings.effect_size: >600,000 single-nucleus transcriptomes profiled
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq of >600,000 nuclei from adult human, chimpanzee, macaque, marmoset dlPFC.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We assessed more than 600,000 single-nucleus transcriptomes from adult human, chimpanzee, macaque, and marmoset dlPFC.
findings.study_system: adult primate dlPFC (human, chimpanzee, macaque, marmoset)

findings.claim: Single-nucleus transcriptomics across 8 human cortical areas shows highly consistent presence of 24 cell subclasses but areal differences in inhibitory-neuron specialization, particularly in primary visual cortex which contains specialized inhibitory interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Primary visual cortex showed characteristic organization with major changes in the excitatory to inhibitory neuron ratio, expansion of layer 4 excitatory neurons, and specialized inhibitory neurons.
findings.effect_size: 24 cell subclasses identified
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq across 8 areas, comparing cell-class proportions and laminar organization.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Single-nucleus RNA-sequencing of 8 areas spanning cortical structural variation showed a highly consistent cellular makeup for 24 cell subclasses.

findings.claim: Human cortical organoids profiled at single-cell, epigenetic, and spatial levels recapitulate endogenous cellular diversification trajectories of the developing human cortex regardless of metabolic state, supporting their use to study fate specification.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that processes of cellular diversification correlate closely to endogenous ones, irrespective of metabolic state, empowering the use of this atlas to study human fate specification.
findings.effect_size: >610,000 cells profiled
findings.evidence: Multimodal atlas of >610,000 cells from human cortical organoid development.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We present a comprehensive single-cell transcriptomic, epigenetic, and spatial atlas of human cortical organoid development, comprising over 610,000 cells, from generation of neural progenitors through production of differentiated neuronal and glial subtypes.

findings.claim: Layer 1 inhibitory interneurons (but not VIP+ cells) are required for tonotopic critical-period map plasticity in primary auditory cortex; their nicotinic-acetylcholine sensitivity is gated developmentally by Lynx1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Silencing of L1 (but not VIP-expressing) cells abolishes map plasticity during the tonotopic critical period.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — abolition of plasticity by L1 silencing
findings.evidence: In vivo silencing experiments and developmental nAChR perturbation in mouse A1.

findings.claim: Single-cell RNA-seq of the human ganglionic eminences identifies regional and temporal progenitor diversity and reveals an evolutionarily conserved transcriptional logic specifying GABAergic projection neurons and interneurons that mirrors rodent programs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that these cells are specified within the human ganglionic eminences by transcriptional programs similar to those previously identified in rodents.
findings.effect_size: qualitative cross-species conservation
findings.evidence: Single-cell RNA-seq of human fetal MGE/LGE/CGE compared to rodent atlases.

findings.claim: Single-nucleus transcriptomic and chromatin-accessibility profiling across prenatal and postnatal human cortex (>700,000 nuclei from 106 donors) defines lineage-specific gene regulatory networks for excitatory neurons, interneurons, glia, and vasculature, and prioritizes interneuron lineages as vulnerable to autism risk.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We analyzed >700,000 single-nucleus RNA sequencing profiles from 106 donors during prenatal and postnatal developmental stages and identified lineage-specific programs that underlie the development of specific subtypes of excitatory cortical neurons, interneurons, glial cell types, and brain vasculature.
findings.effect_size: >700,000 nuclei from 106 donors
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq + snATAC-seq atlas across human cortical development.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We analyzed >700,000 single-nucleus RNA sequencing profiles from 106 donors during prenatal and postnatal developmental stages
findings.study_system: human prenatal and postnatal cortex (106 donors)

findings.claim: A human-specific caudal late interneuron progenitor (CLIP) cell population over-proliferates in tuberous sclerosis complex organoids, generating excess interneurons, brain tumors, and cortical malformations; EGFR inhibition reduces tumor burden.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We generated a human cerebral organoid model for tuberous sclerosis complex (TSC) and identified a specific neural stem cell type, caudal late interneuron progenitor (CLIP) cells.
findings.effect_size: qualitative reduction in tumor burden with EGFR inhibition
findings.evidence: Patient-derived TSC organoids; CLIP cells identified by single-cell transcriptomics; pharmacological EGFR inhibition test.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Epidermal growth factor receptor inhibition reduces tumor burden, identifying potential treatment options for TSC and related disorders.

findings.claim: Cortical interneuron subtype specification is governed by the joint contributions of progenitor spatial origin, birthdate, lineage relationships, and mode of cell division within the ganglionic eminences.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This review will cover recent work that has advanced our knowledge of the mechanisms governing cortical interneuron subtype specification, particularly progenitors' spatial origin, birthdates, lineage, and mode of division.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Synthesis review of GE-derived interneuron specification mechanisms.

findings.claim: Comparative scRNA-seq across macaques and mice reveals that initial prenatal IN classes are largely conserved among mammals; primate-specific TAC3 striatal interneurons arise via a unique progenitor program, and conserved olfactory-bulb-bound precursors are redirected to expanded primate striatum and white matter.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that the initial classes of INs generated prenatally are largely conserved among mammals.
findings.effect_size: 250,181 cells profiled
findings.evidence: Trajectory analysis of 250,181 single cells across macaque and mouse neurogenic ages.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Here, we reconstruct gene expression trajectories specifying INs generated throughout the neurogenic period in macaques and mice by analysing the transcriptomes of 250,181 cells.

findings.claim: Interneuron subtype identity is achieved through interplay of intrinsic genetic programs and activity-dependent interactions with pyramidal cells, with transient developmental connectivity instructing the consolidation of mature subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Accumulating evidence indicates that interneuron identity is achieved through the interplay of intrinsic genetic and activity-dependent programs.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Conceptual review of intrinsic vs. activity-dependent specification mechanisms.

findings.claim: Dlx1/2 and Mash1 (Ascl1) transcription factors operate through parallel and overlapping pathways to specify distinct GABAergic identities and differentiation programs in mouse MGE and CGE progenitors.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This analysis identified Dlx-dependent and Dlx-independent pathways. The Dlx-independent pathway depends in part on the function of the Mash1 basic helix-loop-helix (b-HLH) TF.
findings.effect_size: ~100 transcription factors profiled
findings.evidence: Survey of ~100 transcription factors in MGE/CGE progenitors and assessment of Dlx- and Mash1-mutant mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Here we define the expression of approximately 100 transcription factors (TFs) in progenitors and neurons of the developing mouse medial and caudal ganglionic eminences
findings.study_system: mouse MGE/CGE (transcription factor profiling and mutants)

findings.claim: COUP-TFII (NR2F2) is selectively expressed in CGE-derived hippocampal interneurons (calretinin+, nNOS+, reelin+, neurogliaform, and CCK/calbindin+ basket cells) but is absent from PV+ and SST+ interneurons, supporting CGE lineage as the COUP-TFII+ interneuron population in the adult hippocampus.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the dorsal hippocampal CA1 area, COUP-TFII was restricted to GABAergic interneurons and expressed in several, largely nonoverlapping neuronal populations.
findings.effect_size: ventral > dorsal expression gradient; absent in PV/SST
findings.evidence: Immunohistochemistry, in vivo juxtacellular labeling and theta-rhythm recording in adult rat hippocampus.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Immunoreactivity was present in calretinin-, neuronal nitric oxide synthase-, and reelin-expressing cells, as well as in subsets of cholecystokinin- or calbindin-expressing or radiatum-retrohippocampally projecting GABAergic cells, but not in parvalbumin- and/or somatostatin-expressing interneurons.

findings.claim: Single-cell transcriptomics of Htr3a-GFP+ cortical interneurons identifies three main molecular types with distinct developmental gene-expression dynamics, including a Meis2-defined white-matter interstitial subclass arising from the pallial–subpallial boundary.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We identify three main molecular types of Htr3a-GFP+ interneurons, each displaying distinct developmental dynamics of gene expression.
findings.effect_size: 3 molecular types of Htr3a+ interneurons
findings.evidence: Single-cell RNA-seq and in situ analyses of mouse Htr3a-GFP+ interneurons.

findings.claim: Cortical interneuron specification is driven by transcription factors operating at successive developmental stages, but extrinsic signals also influence fate, indicating an interplay between cell-intrinsic genetic programs and environmental cues.
findings.claim_source_sentence: As genetic programs are influenced by external factors the search continues not only into genetic determinants but also extrinsic influences and the interplay between the two in cell fate specification.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Synthesis of work mapping TF cascades and extrinsic-signal modulation in interneuron specification.

findings.claim: Neurogliaform cells, predominantly CGE-derived, exert distinct rules of inhibitory control over multiple afferent pathways and represent a functionally novel cell type in cortical circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, it is rapidly becoming clear that this cell type operates under a distinct repertoire of rules to provide novel forms of inhibitory control of numerous afferent pathways.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Synthesis of physiology, anatomy, and circuit roles of neurogliaform cells.

findings.claim: Cortical interneuron specification proceeds through three stages — an initial cardinal allocation, definitive subclass refinement after settling in cortex, and final state determination during circuit incorporation — with TF identity arising from configurational rather than combinatorial codes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We present our updated three-stage model for interneuron specification: an initial cardinal step, allocating interneurons into a few major classes, followed by definitive refinement, creating subclasses upon settling within the cortex, and lastly, state determination, reflecting the incorporation of interneurons into functional circuit ensembles.
findings.effect_size: qualitative model
findings.evidence: Synthesis review proposing TF attractor-state model of subtype identity.

findings.claim: Following completion of glutamatergic neurogenesis, mouse neocortical NSCs switch state at ~E16.5 to produce GSX2+ tri-potential intermediate progenitors that generate olfactory-bulb interneurons, cortical oligodendrocytes, and astrocytes; this switch is driven by Sonic hedgehog signaling acting via reduction of GLI3R.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that, at around E16.5, mouse neocortical NSCs start to generate GSX2-expressing (GSX2+) intermediate progenitor cells (IPCs).
findings.effect_size: qualitative; switch occurs at E16.5
findings.evidence: In vivo lineage tracing, conditional Shh manipulation, and single-cell RNA-seq of cortical NSCs.

findings.claim: Postnatal CXCL12 from layer V pyramidal neurons signals through CXCR4/CXCR7 on PV+ basket interneuron axons to promote perisomatic inhibitory synapse formation in mouse mPFC; conditional CXCL12 deletion attenuates inhibitory postsynaptic currents onto layer V pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Conditionally eliminating CXCL12 in neonatal layer V pyramidal neurons led to decreased axon targeting and reduced inhibitory perisomatic synapses from PV+ basket interneurons onto layer V pyramidal neurons.
findings.effect_size: qualitative; reduced perisomatic synapses and attenuated IPSCs
findings.evidence: Conditional Cxcl12 mouse genetics with anatomical and patch-clamp readouts in mPFC.

findings.claim: A subset of cortical first-wave OPCs survives the postnatal apoptotic period and forms preferentially synaptically connected functional clusters with lineage-related GABAergic interneurons, indicating a developmental role of programmed cell death in coordinating shared-lineage cell interactions.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Challenging these ideas, we show that a cortical firstOPC subpopulation survives and forms functional cell clusters with lineage-related interneurons.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — increased synaptic connectivity within shared lineage
findings.evidence: Genetic lineage tracing of first-wave OPCs and lineage-related interneurons with paired electrophysiology.

findings.claim: Clustered γ-Protocadherins (Pcdhgs) act cell-autonomously in mouse GABAergic interneurons to promote survival during the postnatal critical period of programmed cell death; their loss elevates apoptosis, depletes inhibitory populations, and produces seizures, with rescue by BAX deletion.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Conditional deletion of the gene cluster encoding the γ-Protocadherins (Pcdhgs) from developing GABAergic neurons in mice of either sex causes a severe loss of inhibitory populations in multiple brain regions and results in neurologic deficits such as seizures.
findings.effect_size: approximately one-third of GABAergic interneurons normally undergo programmed cell death
findings.evidence: Conditional Pcdhg deletion in interneurons + BAX rescue genetics; cortex and cerebellum analyses.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In the neocortex, inhibitory network formation occurs concurrently with the apoptotic elimination of a third of GABAergic interneurons.

findings.claim: Sp8 and Sp9 are co-expressed in dorsal CGE SVZ and act together — partly by repressing Pak3, Robo1, and Slit1 and by SP8-dependent Cxcl14 expression — to drive development of the ~30% of cortical interneurons derived from the CGE (Htr3a+/CR+/Reelin+/VIP+ subset).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Conditional knockout of Sp8/9 using the Gsx2-Cre transgenic line results in severe loss of CGE-derived cortical interneurons.
findings.effect_size: CGE-derived INs comprise ~30% of all cortical interneurons
findings.evidence: Conditional Gsx2-Cre and Dlx5/6-CIE mouse genetics with marker analysis and downstream-target validation.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: CGE-derived cortical interneurons, which comprise around 30% of all cortical interneurons, mainly express Htr3a, calretinin (CR), Reelin (RELN) and vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP).

findings.claim: Sp9 transcription factor is required in the MGE for normal development of PV+/SST+ cortical interneurons; null/conditional mutants show ~50% reduction in MGE-derived cortical interneurons, ectopic ventral aggregation, and skewed SST+/PV+ ratio, with SP9 directly regulating Arx, Lhx6, Lhx8, Nkx2-1, Zeb2 and the migration genes Ackr3, Epha3 and St18.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Sp9 null and conditional mutant mice have approximately 50% reduction of MGE-derived cortical interneurons, an ectopic aggregation of MGE-derived neurons in the embryonic ventral telencephalon, and an increased ratio of SST+/PV+ cortical interneurons.
findings.effect_size: ~50% reduction in MGE-derived cortical interneurons
findings.evidence: Mouse Sp9 null/conditional genetics, RNA-seq and SP9 ChIP-seq.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Sp9 null and conditional mutant mice have approximately 50% reduction of MGE-derived cortical interneurons

findings.claim: COUP-TFII is broadly expressed in human fetal forebrain GE and neocortex from 9–22 gestational weeks with peak in the CGE and a temporo-occipital > frontal/dorsal gradient; small fractions of COUP-TFII+ cells are proliferating progenitors in the CGE (3.4±0.3%) and cortical VZ/SVZ (1.7±0.1%).
findings.claim_source_sentence: A small fraction of COUP-TFII(+) cells are progenitor cells that proliferate in the CGE (3.4 ± 0.3%) and in the cortical VZ/SVZ (1.7 ± 0.1%).
findings.effect_size: 3.4±0.3% (CGE) and 1.7±0.1% (cortical VZ/SVZ) of COUP-TFII+ cells proliferating
findings.evidence: Immunohistochemistry / double immunofluorescence on human fetal forebrain (9–22 GW).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A small fraction of COUP-TFII(+) cells are progenitor cells that proliferate in the CGE (3.4 ± 0.3%) and in the cortical VZ/SVZ (1.7 ± 0.1%).

findings.claim: COUP-TFII is required in the CGE to specify amygdala (BMA) patterning by directly activating the semaphorin receptors Nrp1 and Nrp2; in conditional mutants, CGE-derived Pax6+ cells fail to populate the basomedial amygdala and lateral/basolateral nuclei are malformed.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Molecular analysis provided evidence that the migration of CGE-derived Pax6(+) cells failed to settle into the BMA nucleus, owing to reduced expression of neuropilin 1 (Nrp1) and Nrp2, two semaphorin receptors that regulate neuronal cell migration and axon guidance.
findings.effect_size: qualitative with direct ChIP-validated targets
findings.evidence: RxCre;COUP-TFII conditional mouse with histology and ChIP for direct targets.

findings.claim: Cortical neurogliaform interneurons originate from a 5-HT3aR+ Hmx3+ pool in the preoptic area, expressing PROX1, NR2F2, and reelin but not VIP, identifying a non-CGE developmental source for the NGC class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using in vivo genetic lineage-tracing in mice, we report that NGCs originate from a specific pool of 5-HT3AR-expressing Hmx3+ cells located in the preoptic area (POA).
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: In vivo genetic lineage tracing combined with molecular, morphological, and electrophysiological characterization in mouse cortex.

findings.claim: Pyramidal-cell-derived Neuregulin 3 acts as a chemoattractive cue via interneuronal ErbB4 to drive cortical-plate invasion and laminar allocation of GABAergic interneurons; perturbation produces abnormal interneuron lamination.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we identified Neuregulin 3 (Nrg3) as a chemoattractive factor expressed by developing pyramidal cells that guides the allocation of cortical interneurons in the developing cortical plate.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — abnormal interneuron lamination
findings.evidence: Nrg3 gain- and loss-of-function in mouse with conditional ErbB4 manipulation.

findings.claim: JNK signaling is required for the tangential-to-radial migration switch and laminar deposition of cortical interneurons; pharmacological or genetic JNK inhibition causes premature stream departure into the cortical plate and abnormal interneuron allocation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Pharmacological inhibition of JNK signaling in ex vivo slice cultures caused cortical interneurons to rapidly depart from migratory streams and prematurely enter the cortical plate.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Ex vivo slice culture with pharmacological inhibition + JNK genetic loss-of-function.

findings.claim: All ventral-eminence-derived cortical interneurons (including CGE-derived) undergo programmed cell death, but in contrast to MGE/PV/SST classes, VIP interneuron survival is NOT activity-dependent; Calcineurin in interneurons sequentially controls morphological maturation (E15–P5) and subsequent activity-dependent survival (P5–P20).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Moreover, with the exception of VIP interneurons, this occurs in a manner that is activity-dependent.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — VIP interneurons are exception
findings.evidence: Cell-type-specific genetic and activity manipulations in mouse cortex with cell-death and morphology readouts.

findings.claim: Selective loss of MeCP2 from VIP+ cortical interneurons in mice replicates key neural and behavioral phenotypes of global MeCP2 deficiency, identifying CGE-derived VIP interneurons as a developmental locus of vulnerability in Rett Syndrome.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we find that loss of MeCP2 specifically from VIP interneurons replicates key neural and behavioral phenotypes observed following global Mecp2 loss of function.
findings.effect_size: qualitative
findings.evidence: Conditional VIP-Cre Mecp2 deletion mouse with electrophysiology and behavioral assays.

findings.claim: Postmitotic deletion of Dicer in mouse VIP+ interneurons concordantly disrupts intrinsic and synaptic properties of VIP cells and connected pyramidal neurons, then triggers progressive VIP+ interneuron loss with elevated cortical LFP power, demonstrating CGE-lineage-specific miRNA dependence.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we conditionally removed Dicer in postmitotic VIP+ interneurons to block miRNA biogenesis.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — progressive VIP+ loss
findings.evidence: Conditional VIP-Cre;Dicer mouse with electrophysiology, in vivo LFP and behavior.

findings.claim: Id2 marks neocortical inhibitory interneurons that do not express PV, SST, or VIP — including non-VIP cells in L1, neurogliaform cells in L2–6, and a variety of CCK interneurons — and the majority of L2–6 Id2 INs are NPY+ late-spiking neurogliaform cells with broad local connectivity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we show that Id2 is a marker for the INs in the neocortex that do not express PV, SST or VIP, including the non-VIP cells in L1, NGFCs in L2-6 and a variety of CCK INs.
findings.effect_size: qualitative — fourth major cortical inhibitory class
findings.evidence: Id2-Cre intersectional genetics, slice electrophysiology, in vivo silicon probe recordings.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Using intersectional genetic strategies to label and optogenetically stimulate Id2 INs, we find that the vast majority of Id2 INs in L2-6 are NPY-expressing NGFCs that exhibit the late-spiking firing pattern and broad connectivity characteristic of these cells.

findings.claim: Six transcriptomic subclasses of GABAergic neurons (Sst, Pvalb, Vip, Lamp5, Sncg, Serpinf1) plus two distinct types (Sst-Chodl, Meis2-Adamts19) define mouse adult cortical interneuron taxonomy by single-cell RNA-seq, with the major division corresponding to MGE origin (Pvalb, Sst) versus CGE origin (Lamp5, Sncg, Serpinf1, Vip).
findings.claim_source_sentence: GABAergic cell type taxonomy by scRNA-seq We define six subclasses of GABAergic cells: Sst, Pvalb, Vip , Lamp5, Sncg and Serpinf1 , and two distinct types: Sst-Chodl and Meis2-Adamts19 ( Fig. 1c ). The major division among GABAergic types largely corresponds to their developmental origin in the medial ganglionic eminence ( Pvalb and Sst subclasses) or caudal ganglionic eminence (Lamp5, Sncg, Serpinfl and Vip subclasses).
findings.effect_size: 6 GABAergic subclasses + 2 distinct types; nearly all GABA types shared across V1 and ALM (vs. glutamatergic types mostly area-specific)
findings.evidence: 23,822 cells from mouse primary visual cortex (VISp) and anterior lateral motor cortex (ALM); 133 transcriptomic types defined by scRNA-seq.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Nearly all types of GABA (γ-aminobutyric acid)-containing neurons are shared across both areas, whereas most types of glutamatergic neurons were found in one of the two areas.

figure_data.context: Plus 2 distinct types (Sst-Chodl, Meis2-Adamts19)

findings.claim: Postmitotic ganglionic-eminence cells diverge into transcriptionally distinct precursor states corresponding to non-overlapping cardinal interneuron classes (Pvalb, Sst, Vip, Id2, Th, Nos1, Igfbp6), with Vip and Id2 cardinal markers (Vip/Synpr/Igf1, Reln/Mpped1/Id2) becoming statistically detectable by E18.5.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These could be allocated into non-overlapping cardinal types of cortical interneurons (Pvalb, Sst, Vip, Id2, Th, Nos1, Igfbp6). A minority of E13.5 cells also mapped to Vip and Id2 subsets, but conserved transcriptomic markers did not pass statistical significance until E18.5 (E18.5 markers of Vip neurons: Vip , Synpr , Igf1 ; E18.5 markers of Id2 neurons: Reln , Mpped1, Id2 ).
findings.effect_size: 7 cardinal classes; Vip-cardinal markers detectable by E18.5; clear bipolar vs multipolar Vip segregation by P10
findings.evidence: Single-cell RNA-seq across mouse developmental timecourse, integrated MGE (E13.5)+CGE/LGE (E14.5) progenitors with postnatal P10 reference; cardinal-type segregation of Sst-Martinotti vs non-Martinotti, Vip bipolar vs multipolar visible by P10.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Based on this alignment, P10 cells exhibited strong evidence of transcriptomic separation beyond cardinal types, ( Fig. 4B ), including clear segregation between Sst Martinotti versus non-Martinotti (X94), Vip bipolar versus multipolar, and Id2 neurogliaform versus non-neurogliaform interneuron subtypes ( Fig. 4E ; Extended Data Fig. 7 – 9 ).
findings.study_system: mouse, GE (E13.5 MGE / E14.5 CGE+LGE) → P10 cortex scRNA-seq

figure_data.metric: earliest E-stage with statistically conserved Vip cardinal markers
figure_data.context: Vip markers Vip/Synpr/Igf1; Id2 markers Reln/Mpped1/Id2

findings.claim: Mef2c expression delineates early Pvalb-precursors among E13.5 MGE postmitotic cells and is essential for their development, identifying an autism-spectrum-disorder-associated transcription factor as a determinant of MGE-derived interneuron specification.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our analysis revealed that the ASD-associated transcription factor Mef2c delineates early Pvalb-precursors, and is essential for their development.
findings.effect_size: Mef2c required for early Pvalb-lineage development
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq of E13.5 MGE plus Mef2c conditional manipulation; identified Mef2c, Erbb4, Plcxd3 as early Pvalb-precursor markers, distinct from Sst-precursors (Sst, Tspan7, Satb1).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Examining the earliest stages, we observed a separation of Pvalb- and Sst-precursor cells within the E13.5 postmitotic populations ( Fig. 4D ), and identified transcriptomic markers that were conserved into adulthood (Early marker genes for Pvalb neurons: Mef2c, Erbb4, Plcxd3; Early marker genes for Sst neurons: Sst, Tspan7, Satb1; Fig.4E ; Extended Data Fig. 7 ).

findings.claim: All neocortical VIP-expressing GABAergic interneurons fall within the 5HT3aR-expressing group; a roughly equally numerous 5HT3aR-positive but VIP-negative subgroup that includes neurogliaform cells (reelin-positive) accounts for the rest of the 5HT3aR class, which itself constitutes ~30% of cortical interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The 5HT3aR group, which also accounts for ~30% of the total interneuronal population, is heterogeneous and includes all of the neurons that express the neuropeptide VIP, as well as an equally numerous subgroup of neurons that do not express VIP and includes neurogliaform cells.
findings.effect_size: 5HT3aR group ≈ 30% of cortical interneurons; ~50% of these are VIP+ and ~50% VIP-/reelin+/neurogliaform-type
findings.evidence: Review of multiple molecular profiling and 5HT3a-Cre fate-mapping studies; PV (~40%), SST (~30%), 5HT3aR (~30%) collectively account for nearly 100% of neocortical interneurons in mouse somatosensory cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The PV group accounts for ~40% of GABAergic neurons and includes fast spiking basket cells and chandelier cells. The SST group, which represents ~30% of GABAergic neurons, includes the Martinotti cells and a set of neurons that specifically target layer IV. The 5HT3aR group, which also accounts for ~30% of the total interneuronal population, is heterogeneous and includes all of the neurons that express the neuropeptide VIP, as well as an equally numerous subgroup of neurons that do not express VIP and includes neurogliaform cells.

figure_data.context: remaining ~30% SST + ~30% 5HT3aR (all VIP+)
encompasses all VIP+ plus ~equal VIP-/reelin+ neurogliaform cells

findings.claim: The transcriptomic Pvalb-Vipr2 type (previously Pvalb-Cpne5) corresponds to cortical chandelier cells, while a transcriptomically distinct chandelier-like type (CHC2) maps to Lamp5-Lhx6, which is most closely related to Lamp5 neurogliaform types — establishing two molecularly distinct chandelier-cell-like populations across MGE-derived (Pvalb) and CGE-related (Lamp5-Lhx6) lineages.
findings.claim_source_sentence: For the Pvalb subclass, we confirm that the Pvalb-Vipr2 type (Pvalb-Cpne5 in our previous study 20 ), corresponds to chandelier cells by mapping of the recently reported chandelier cell (CHC1) RNA-seq data 36 to our Pvalb-Vipr2 type ( Extended Data Fig. 12a ). We find that the RNA-seq data of chandelier type 2 cells (CHC2) 36 map primarily to our Lamp5-Lhx6 type ( Extended Data Fig. 12a, b ), which is transcriptomically most related to Lamp5 neurogliaform types.
findings.effect_size: 2 chandelier-related transcriptomic types (Pvalb-Vipr2 and Lamp5-Lhx6) across MGE/CGE divide
findings.evidence: Cross-mapping of published chandelier-cell scRNA-seq (Paul et al CHC1/CHC2) onto the 133-type Tasic atlas; new Vipr2-IRES2-cre line confirms Pvalb-Vipr2 chandelier identity without tamoxifen.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We used the new genetic marker Vipr2 to develop Vipr2-IRES2-cre to access chandelier cells (Extended Data Figs. 8 , 13a–f ).

findings.claim: The caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE) is the developmental origin of all VIP-expressing cortical interneurons and at least 90% of 5HT3aR-expressing cortical interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In their characterization of the interneurons that originate in the caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE), where all VIP and at least 90% of 5HT3aR neurons originate (see below), Myoshi et al. (2010) described nine electrophysiologically different subtypes, of which six accounted for >93% of the CGE population.
findings.effect_size: ≥90% of 5HT3aR cortical interneurons CGE-derived; 100% of VIP cortical interneurons CGE-derived
findings.evidence: Genetic fate mapping with 5HT3a-Cre/Htr3a-BACEGFP and CGE region-specific markers in mouse embryos; nine electrophysiological subtypes characterized at adulthood, six accounting for >93% of CGE-derived population.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In their characterization of the interneurons that originate in the caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE), where all VIP and at least 90% of 5HT3aR neurons originate (see below), Myoshi et al. (2010) described nine electrophysiologically different subtypes, of which six accounted for >93% of the CGE population.
findings.study_system: mouse, CGE-derived cortical interneurons (review citing Miyoshi 2010)

figure_data.context: remaining ~10% from MGE/preoptic area

figure_data.metric: % cortical VIP interneurons originating in CGE

findings.claim: Two electrophysiologically distinct 'late-spiking' CGE-derived 5HT3aR multipolar interneuron subtypes (LS1/LS2; also termed dense plexus and initial-adapting cells) co-express reelin but not VIP, providing a molecular/physiological signature for non-VIP 5HT3aR interneurons.
