findings.claim_source_sentence: Studies of CGE-derived and 5HT3aR interneurons have described two types of "late-spiking" cells, both of which are multipolar, express reelin but not VIP, and have been termed LS1 and LS2 (also described as "dense plexus cells" and initial adapting) ( Butt et al., 2005 ; Miyoshi et al., 2007 ; Lee et al., 2010 ; Miyoshi et al., 2010 ).
findings.effect_size: 2 late-spiking subtypes (LS1/LS2) within CGE-derived 5HT3aR/Reln+/VIP- population
findings.evidence: Multiple studies (Butt 2005, Miyoshi 2007/2010, Lee 2010) characterizing CGE-derived 5HT3aR interneurons by morphology and physiology in mouse cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Studies of CGE-derived and 5HT3aR interneurons have described two types of "late-spiking" cells, both of which are multipolar, express reelin but not VIP, and have been termed LS1 and LS2 (also described as "dense plexus cells" and initial adapting) ( Butt et al., 2005 ; Miyoshi et al., 2007 ; Lee et al., 2010 ; Miyoshi et al., 2010 ).

findings.claim: While the medial ganglionic eminence (MGE) produces parvalbumin and somatostatin cardinal classes, the caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE) produces the rarer neurogliaform, bipolar, and VIP-expressing multipolar interneuron classes; in cortex these neurogliaform neurons are CGE-derived, while in hippocampus most arise from MGE — illustrating region-specific lineage allocation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The CGE produces the relatively rarer subtypes including the neurogliaform, bipolar and VIP-expressing multipolar interneurons 12 . For instance, while in the cortex neurogliaform neurons are CGE-derived, a large proportion of the corresponding population in the hippocampus arises from the MGE 19 .
findings.effect_size: CGE-derived: neurogliaform + bipolar + VIP multipolar (cortex); MGE: PV + SST cardinal classes
findings.evidence: Review synthesis of fate-mapping (Lhx6-Cre, Nkx2.1-Cre, Mash1-CreER, 5HT3a-Cre) studies in mouse forebrain.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Virtually all GABAergic interneurons within the telencephalon arise from one of two embryonic subcortical progenitor zones, the medial and caudal ganglionic eminences (MGE and CGE, Figure 2 ).

findings.claim: Almost all GABAergic interneurons in cortical layer I (which contains only inhibitory neurons, no excitatory cells) express 5HT3aR, indicating that layer I interneurons are predominantly CGE-derived.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Almost all neurons in layer I, a layer that only contains GABAergic interneurons, are 5HT3aR-expressing.
findings.effect_size: ≈100% of layer I GABAergic interneurons are 5HT3aR-positive
findings.evidence: Double in situ hybridization (GAD67 + 5HT3aR/PV/SST) on mouse primary somatosensory cortex; quantitative laminar distribution.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Almost all neurons in layer I, a layer that only contains GABAergic interneurons, are 5HT3aR-expressing.

figure_data.context: layer I contains only GABAergic interneurons in cortex

findings.claim: Genetic inducible fate mapping with the L/CGE-specific Mash1-BAC CreER mouse line shows nearly complete overlap with 5HT3aR-expressing cortical interneurons, confirming the CGE as the major embryonic source of cortical 5HT3aR/VIP-lineage interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The second strategy used the L/CGE-specific Mash1-BAC CreER ; R26R tdRFP mouse line showing a nearly complete overlap with 5HT3aR cells confirming the CGE as the major source of this population.
findings.effect_size: Near-complete (≈100%) overlap of Mash1-CreER fate-map with 5HT3aR+ cortical interneurons
findings.evidence: GIFM (genetic inducible fate mapping) using Mash1-BAC CreER × R26R tdRFP cross; complementary Nkx2.1-BAC cre experiment showed no overlap with 5HT3aR cells, excluding MGE.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The second strategy used the L/CGE-specific Mash1-BAC CreER ; R26R tdRFP mouse line showing a nearly complete overlap with 5HT3aR cells confirming the CGE as the major source of this population.
findings.study_system: mouse, embryonic GE → adult cortex fate mapping

findings.claim: The preoptic area (POA), distinct from MGE and CGE, contributes a small homogeneous population of NPY-positive multipolar interneuron-selective interneurons that lack PV, SST, calretinin and VIP markers and predominantly populate superficial cortical layers including layer I.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recently, it was confirmed, using GIFM with an Nkx5-1 cre line, that the POA also gives rise to a small and seemingly homogenous group of multipolar IS cortical interneurons mostly populating superficial layers (including layer I) that express NPY but not any of the interneuron markers PV, SST, CR,
findings.effect_size: Small POA-derived population; multipolar; NPY+, IS-type; superficial-layer biased
findings.evidence: Genetic inducible fate mapping using Nkx5-1-Cre in mouse embryos; reviewed alongside CGE and MGE fate-mapping data.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Recently, it was confirmed, using GIFM with an Nkx5-1 cre line, that the POA also gives rise to a small and seemingly homogenous group of multipolar IS cortical interneurons mostly populating superficial layers (including layer I) that express NPY but not any of the interneuron markers PV, SST, CR,

findings.claim: All three ganglionic eminences (MGE, CGE, LGE) share a highly conserved transcriptomic maturation trajectory through mitotic progenitors, while eminence-specific transcription factor expression — overlaid on this conserved trajectory — seeds the emergence of cardinal interneuron diversity at the postmitotic stage.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Heterogeneity within mitotic progenitors in the ganglionic eminences is driven by a highly conserved maturation trajectory, alongside eminence-specific transcription factor expression that seeds the emergence of later diversity. Upon becoming postmitotic, progenitors diverge and differentiate into transcriptionally distinct states, including an interneuron precursor state.
findings.effect_size: Single shared maturation trajectory across all three GE; eminence-specific TFs only after diverging into precursor states
findings.evidence: Drop-seq scRNA-seq of dissected E13.5 MGE and E14.5 CGE+LGE from mouse embryos; principal-curve maturation trajectory; PCA + reverse graph embedding cross-validation.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Within mitotic progenitors, we found a highly conserved maturation trajectory, accompanied by eminence-specific transcription factor expression that seeds the emergence of later cell diversity.
findings.study_system: mouse, embryonic GE (E13.5 MGE / E14.5 CGE+LGE)

findings.claim: Lhx6-GFP-negative cells dissected from CGE/LGE produce diverse Vip-, Id2-, and Sncg-aligned postmitotic precursor states distinct from Lhx6-GFP-positive MGE-derived cells, allowing dissection of the CGE-specific developmental trajectory; FACS-purification with stringent Lhx6+ filtering (≥20% Lhx6 mRNA threshold) confirms the CGE specificity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: H) Our Lhx6-GFP-negative dataset contains both mitotic and postmitotic cells from the CGE and diffusion map analysis shows our previously defined maturation trajectory. K) To avoid the possibility of FACS false negative MGE cells contaminating our Lhx6-GFP-negative dataset, we clustered the postmitotic cells from this dataset, and filtered out three rare clusters where Lhx6 mRNA expression was detected in more than 20% of cells ( Supplementary Methods ).
findings.effect_size: 20% Lhx6 mRNA threshold for CGE-specific clusters; clear MGE/CGE postmitotic separation
findings.evidence: Lhx6-GFP transgenic mouse line, FACS-purified GFP-positive (MGE) and GFP-negative (CGE/LGE) cells; diffusion map analysis; clustering with Lhx6 contamination filtering.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: K) To avoid the possibility of FACS false negative MGE cells contaminating our Lhx6-GFP-negative dataset, we clustered the postmitotic cells from this dataset, and filtered out three rare clusters where Lhx6 mRNA expression was detected in more than 20% of cells ( Supplementary Methods ).

findings.claim: Olig2-CreER-based temporal genetic fate mapping in mouse MGE shows that physiological subtypes of cortical interneurons are largely unique to their birth time point — establishing time of neurogenesis within the MGE as a major determinant of interneuron physiological identity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We used an inducible form of Cre under the regulation of Olig2, a basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor highly expressed in neural progenitors of the MGE. We observe that the physiological subtypes of cortical interneurons are, to a large degree, unique to their time point of generation.
findings.effect_size: Physiological subtypes (FS, RSNP, IS, etc.) largely segregated by birthdate within the MGE
findings.evidence: Tamoxifen-inducible Olig2-CreER × Cre-dependent reporter mice; tamoxifen administration at successive embryonic timepoints (E10.5–E15.5); slice electrophysiology of cortical interneurons in adult.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We observe that the physiological subtypes of cortical interneurons are, to a large degree, unique to their time point of generation.
findings.study_system: mouse, MGE Olig2-CreER inducible fate mapping

findings.claim: Distinct subdomains of the embryonic subpallial neuroepithelium generate non-overlapping cortical interneuron subtypes: calbindin/PV/SST interneurons exclusively from Lhx6-expressing MGE precursors; Martinotti cells co-expressing calretinin/SST from the dorsal Nkx6.2-expressing MGE; and most NPY/bipolar calretinin interneurons from Gsh2-expressing LGE/CGE germinal zones — formally linking molecular subdomain identity to adult interneuron subtype.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical interneurons expressing calbindin, parvalbumin, and somatostatin are generated exclusively from Lhx6 (Lim homeobox 6)-expressing precursors in the medial ganglionic eminence (MGE). Martinotti cells that coexpress calretinin and somatostatin are generated from the dorsal region of the MGE neuroepithelium that expresses Nkx6.2 (NK2 transcription factor-related 6.2). Most neuropeptide Y-expressing cells and all bipolar calretinin-expressing interneurons are generated outside the MGE, from the germinal zones of the lateral/caudal ganglionic eminences that express Gsh2 (genomic screened homeobox 2).
findings.effect_size: 100% of cortical PV/SST/calbindin INs from Lhx6+ MGE; ≈100% of bipolar CR+ INs from Gsh2+ L/CGE
findings.evidence: Cre–lox transgenic mice with Lhx6-Cre, Nkx6.2-Cre, Gsh2-Cre and reporters; immunohistochemistry for PV, SST, calbindin, calretinin, NPY in adult cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Most neuropeptide Y-expressing cells and all bipolar calretinin-expressing interneurons are generated outside the MGE, from the germinal zones of the lateral/caudal ganglionic eminences that express Gsh2 (genomic screened homeobox 2).
findings.study_system: mouse, embryonic subpallium → adult cortex fate mapping

findings.claim: Loss of both DLX-1 and DLX-2 homeobox proteins eliminates detectable cell migration from the subcortical telencephalon into the developing neocortex and reduces neocortical GABA-expressing cells, providing the founding genetic evidence that cortical GABAergic interneurons originate subcortically and require Dlx genes for tangential migration.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Mice lacking the homeodomain proteins DLX-1 and DLX-2 show no detectable cell migration from the subcortical telencephalon to the neocortex and also have few GABA-expressing cells in the neocortex.
findings.effect_size: Complete loss of subcortex→neocortex migration in Dlx1/2 dKO; large reduction in cortical GABA cells
findings.evidence: Dlx1/Dlx2 double-knockout mouse embryos; in vitro slice migration assays; GABA immunostaining in neocortical slices ± subcortical telencephalon attachment.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Mice lacking the homeodomain proteins DLX-1 and DLX-2 show no detectable cell migration from the subcortical telencephalon to the neocortex and also have few GABA-expressing cells in the neocortex.
findings.study_system: mouse, Dlx1/Dlx2 double knockout, embryonic telencephalon

findings.claim: Cortical apical neuroprogenitors transition through successive transcriptional states across mouse embryonic development, generating daughter neurons whose differentiation programs reflect parental progenitor state at the time of birth — establishing a 'temporally patterned' progenitor model in which a parentally-supplied transcriptional template is overlaid on a postmitotic differentiation program to produce neocortical cell-type diversity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Telley et al. used single-cell RNA sequencing to survey the transcriptional identity of cells early in mouse brain development. As a neuroprogenitor transitioned to new states, it produced daughter neurons that reflect those new states. The neuron's own postmitotic differentiation program is apparently overlaid onto these parentally supplied programs, driving emergence of specialized neuronal cell types in the neocortex.
findings.effect_size: Progenitor state at neurogenesis time predicts daughter-neuron transcriptional identity
findings.evidence: Single-cell RNA-seq surveying mouse cortical progenitors and immediate daughter neurons across embryonic timepoints; analysis of progenitor-state-to-daughter transcriptomic correlation.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: As a neuroprogenitor transitioned to new states, it produced daughter neurons that reflect those new states.
findings.study_system: mouse, embryonic cortical progenitors (apical, ventricular zone)

findings.claim: Selective loss of Dlx1 (sparing Dlx2) in mice causes subtype-specific reduction of cortical and hippocampal interneurons including calretinin-, somatostatin- and neuropeptide Y-positive subsets, producing disinhibition and adult-onset epilepsy — implicating Dlx1 in long-term maintenance/specification of specific interneuron subtypes after migration is complete.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Mice lacking Dlx1 show subtype-specific loss of interneurons, reduced inhibition and epilepsy
findings.effect_size: Selective subset (CR/SST/NPY) loss; reduced GABAergic inhibition; spontaneous seizures
findings.evidence: Dlx1-/- mice (vs Dlx1/2 dKO that block migration); immunohistochemistry quantification of CR/SST/NPY interneurons; cortical/hippocampal slice electrophysiology; EEG recordings of seizures.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Mice lacking Dlx1 show subtype-specific loss of interneurons, reduced inhibition and epilepsy

findings.claim: Single-cell RNA-seq of developing human telencephalon reveals discrete cell types and temporally/spatially restricted radial glia and neurogenesis trajectories, with mTOR signaling enriched in outer radial glia and a mixed model of topographical, typological and temporal hierarchies governing cell-type diversification across rostral vs. caudal cerebral cortex — providing a baseline for understanding human cortical interneuron diversification context.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We identified discrete cell types as well as temporally and spatially restricted trajectories of radial glia maturation and neurogenesis in developing human telencephalon. These lineage-specific trajectories reveal the expression of neurogenic transcription factors in early radial glia and enriched activation of mammalian target of rapamycin signaling in outer radial glia.
findings.effect_size: Mixed topographical/typological/temporal hierarchies; oRG-specific mTOR enrichment
findings.evidence: Single-cell RNA-seq of human developing telencephalon spanning multiple regions and stages; lineage trajectory inference; mTOR pathway enrichment.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Across cortical areas, modest transcriptional differences among radial glia cascade into robust typological distinctions among maturing neurons.

findings.claim: Human middle temporal gyrus contains roughly equal proportions of MGE-derived (44% LHX6+) and CGE-derived (50% ADARB2+) cortical interneurons by single-nucleus RNA-seq, in stark contrast to mouse cortex where MGE-derived interneurons constitute ~70% and CGE-derived ~30% — establishing a major species-specific shift toward CGE-lineage interneuron expansion in primates/humans.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Human MTG had similar proportions of MGE (44% LHX6+ nuclei) and CGE (50% ADARB2+ nuclei) interneurons based on snRNA-seq data. In contrast, prior studies report ~70% MGE versus ~30% CGE interneurons in mouse cortex 38 , 42 .
findings.effect_size: Human: 44% MGE / 50% CGE; Mouse: ~70% MGE / ~30% CGE
findings.evidence: Single-nucleus RNA-seq of ~16,000 nuclei from layer-dissected human middle temporal gyrus; clustered into 75 cell types including 45 GABAergic; LHX6 vs ADARB2 expression assigned MGE vs CGE lineage.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Human MTG had similar proportions of MGE (44% LHX6+ nuclei) and CGE (50% ADARB2+ nuclei) interneurons based on snRNA-seq data.
findings.study_system: human, middle temporal gyrus (adult); compared to mouse cortex literature

figure_data.context: vs ~70% in mouse cortex
vs ~30% in mouse cortex

findings.claim: VIP is the most transcriptomically diverse interneuron subclass in human middle temporal gyrus (21 types), with many VIP types enriched in upper layers and several closely related to a layer 1–2 LAMP5/PAX6 (L1 LAMP5 NMBR) type — indicating extensive intra-VIP molecular heterogeneity in human cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP was the most diverse subclass (21 types), with many types enriched in upper layers ( Fig. 3a ). Several VIP types were closely related to the LAMP5/PAX6 type L1 LAMP5 NMBR and localized to L1-L2.
findings.effect_size: 21 transcriptomically distinct VIP types in human MTG (vs fewer in mouse)
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq of human MTG (8 donors, ~16,000 nuclei); 45 GABAergic types parsed; ADARB2-branch organization with LAMP5/PAX6 vs VIP subclasses.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP was the most diverse subclass (21 types), with many types enriched in upper layers ( Fig. 3a ).

figure_data.metric: Number of transcriptomic VIP types in human MTG
figure_data.context: Most diverse interneuron subclass; many enriched in upper layers

findings.claim: The mouse CGE marker HTR3A (5HT3aR) is NOT expressed in human CGE-lineage interneuron types, marking a major species-specific divergence in CGE molecular signature and indicating that mouse VIP/CGE markers cannot be transferred unchanged to human cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Surprisingly, the mouse CGE interneuron marker HTR3A 38 was not expressed in human CGE types ( Fig. 3c ).
findings.effect_size: Human CGE types lack HTR3A expression vs near-universal expression in mouse CGE-derived 5HT3aR group
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq of human MTG; cross-species marker comparison with published mouse cortex data; cell-type tree alignment via MetaNeighbor and conserved/divergent marker analysis.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Surprisingly, the mouse CGE interneuron marker HTR3A 38 was not expressed in human CGE types ( Fig. 3c ).
findings.study_system: human (MTG snRNA-seq) vs. mouse CGE literature

findings.claim: Some CGE-derived cell types in human cortical layer 1 express SST (somatostatin), a feature not seen in mouse layer 1 interneurons — providing a primate/human-specific anatomical-molecular feature distinguishing CGE-derived interneurons from rodents.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Some CGE-derived cell types in L1 expressed SST ( Fig. 3a , c ), as described in human 14 but not in mouse L1 interneurons 22 .
findings.effect_size: Human L1 contains CGE-derived SST+ types; mouse L1 lacks SST+ interneurons
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq of human MTG with layer-dissected nuclei; SST expression in CGE-derived (ADARB2-branch) L1 types; cross-reference to prior human histological studies.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Some CGE-derived cell types in L1 expressed SST ( Fig. 3a , c ), as described in human 14 but not in mouse L1 interneurons 22 .
findings.study_system: human MTG L1 (snRNA-seq) vs. mouse L1 literature

findings.claim: A 1.3-million-cell scRNA-seq taxonomy of adult mouse isocortex and hippocampal formation organizes GABAergic interneurons into two developmental-origin neighborhoods (CGE and MGE), with the CGE neighborhood divided into Lamp5, Sncg and Vip subclasses (4, 5, and 6 supertypes respectively) and the MGE neighborhood into Sst Chodl, Sst, and Pvalb (1, 11, and 3 supertypes); identifies six Vip supertypes as the developmentally CGE-derived Vip lineage backbone.
findings.claim_source_sentence: GABAergic cell type taxonomy The GABAergic inhibitory neuronal class is divided into two neighborhoods that correlate with distinct developmental origins: caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE) ( Fig. 2A – E ) and medial ganglionic eminence (MGE) ( Fig. 2F – J ). Each neighborhood is further divided into 3 subclasses: Lamp5, Sncg and Vip in CGE, and Sst Chodl, Sst and Pvalb in MGE. In the CGE neighborhood, the Lamp5 (mostly neurogliaform cells), Sncg and Vip subclasses are divided into 4, 5 (one containing Vip cells), and 6 supertypes, respectively ( Fig. 2D , Table S3 ).
findings.effect_size: 6 Vip supertypes (CGE), 5 Sncg supertypes, 4 Lamp5 supertypes
findings.evidence: ~1.3 million cells from entire adult mouse isocortex and HPF profiled by 10x scRNA-seq; integrated UMAP and hierarchical clustering; supertype/subclass nomenclature.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In the CGE neighborhood, the Lamp5 (mostly neurogliaform cells), Sncg and Vip subclasses are divided into 4, 5 (one containing Vip cells), and 6 supertypes, respectively ( Fig. 2D , Table S3 ).

figure_data.metric: Number of CGE Vip supertypes in mouse cortex+HPF
figure_data.context: out of 1.3M cells; CGE neighborhood = Lamp5/Sncg/Vip

findings.claim: Some cortical 'CGE-classified' neurogliaform cells are in fact developmentally derived from the nearby preoptic region (POA) rather than CGE proper, and a hippocampal-specific Ntng1 HPF supertype lacks canonical pan-CGE Prox1 expression — refining the developmental boundaries of the CGE-defined interneuron lineage.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Note that some of the CGE cell types (e.g., some neurogliaform cells) may, in fact, be developmentally derived from the nearby preoptic region (PO) ( Niquille et al., 2018 ). For CGE, the Ntng1 HPF supertype does not express canonical pan-CGE marker Prox1 ( Miyoshi et al., 2015 ; Rubin and Kessaris, 2013 ) nor its subclass markers Vip, Sncg or Lamp5 ( Fig. 2A ).
findings.effect_size: Subset of cortical NGFCs from POA; Ntng1 HPF supertype Prox1-negative
findings.evidence: Cross-reference of taxonomy with developmental fate-mapping literature (Niquille 2018 POA-derived NGFC; Miyoshi 2015 Prox1 fate map).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Note that some of the CGE cell types (e.g., some neurogliaform cells) may, in fact, be developmentally derived from the nearby preoptic region (PO) ( Niquille et al., 2018 ).

findings.claim: A 'Lamp5 Lhx6' supertype straddles the canonical CGE/MGE division by expressing both the CGE-class Lamp5 marker and the MGE-class Lhx6 transcription factor, paralleling reports in human cortex (LAMP5/PAX6 Inh L2-6 LAMP5 CA1) and supporting that some Lamp5-class interneurons may have MGE developmental origin contrary to canonical CGE-only assignment.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These include the Lamp5 Lhx6, Ntng1 HPF, Vip Cbln4 HPF, Sst Lmo1 HPF and Sst Ctsc HPF supertypes, and select clusters within shared supertypes ( Fig. 2A , D , F , I ).
findings.effect_size: Lamp5 Lhx6 supertype: CGE-marker + MGE-TF expression in same cells
findings.evidence: Identification of Lamp5+/Lhx6+ supertype within Yao 2021 mouse atlas; cross-validation with Hodge 2019 human MTG snRNA-seq finding of LAMP5 Inh L2-6 LAMP5 CA1 type expressing LHX6.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Among LAMP5/PAX6 types, only Inh L2-6 LAMP5 CA1 expressed LHX6 , suggesting possible origins in MGE like Lamp5 Lhx6 cells described in mouse 22 .
findings.study_system: mouse cortex (Yao 2021); replicated in human MTG (Hodge 2019)

findings.claim: Knock-in of Cre recombinase at the endogenous Vip locus (Vip-IRES-Cre mouse) faithfully labels CGE-derived cortical VIP-expressing interneurons that do not overlap with PV or SST populations and selectively innervate other interneurons (interneuron-selective / disinhibitory function), establishing a primary genetic tool for VIP-lineage circuit analysis.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The VIP-ires-Cre targets interneurons which selectively innervate other interneurons Vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) is expressed in a subset of cortical GABAergic neurons that are derived from the caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE) and do not overlap with SST or PV interneurons (Miyoshi et al., 2010b).
findings.effect_size: Vip-IRES-Cre labels CGE-derived VIP+ INs; no overlap with SST/PV; selectively innervates other interneurons
findings.evidence: Knock-in IRES-Cre at Vip locus in mouse; characterization in neocortex, hippocampus, olfactory bulb, suprachiasmatic nucleus by reporter crossing (Ai9, Ai14); slice electrophysiology in visual cortex; immunohistochemistry confirming PV/SST/CR/VIP marker overlap.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In the VIP-ires-Cre driver, Cre activity is detected in the neocortex, hippocampus, olfactory bulb, suprachiasmatic nuclei, and other discrete midbrain and brain stem regions ( Sppl Figure 5 ).

findings.claim: In mid-gestational human fetal brain, an interneuron-associated co-expression module (C31) is enriched in cortical subgranular and ventricular zones with DLX1 and DLX2 as central hub genes, and is one of only two modules (out of 42 tested) enriched for human-accelerated conserved non-coding sequences (haCNSs) — implying species-specific regulation of human cortical interneuron specification/migration.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Finally, we identified a module (C31) with particular enrichment in SG and VZ ( Fig. 3b , upper right), containing many interneuron-associated genes ( Fig. 3c ). DLX1 and DLX2, homeobox transcription factors essential for interneuron migration and survival 10 , were central ("hub") genes therein. Only two of the 42 tested modules were enriched for haCNSs: the interneuron-related (C31) and SP-enriched (C16) modules, which both mark processes with features potentially distinct to the human lineage.
findings.effect_size: C31 module: DLX1/DLX2 hubs; 1 of 2 (out of 42) modules with haCNS enrichment
findings.evidence: Microarray transcriptomic profiling of mid-gestational human brain across regions and layers (LMD-dissected); WGCNA module identification (42 modules); haCNS enrichment test.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Only two of the 42 tested modules were enriched for haCNSs: the interneuron-related (C31) and SP-enriched (C16) modules, which both mark processes with features potentially distinct to the human lineage.

findings.claim: Mid-gestational human brain transcriptional profiling notes species differences in the developmental origin of cortical GABAergic interneurons compared to rodents, with GABAergic-interneuron and proliferative gene sets highly expressed across all three human ganglionic eminences (LGE, MGE, CGE) — informing debate about whether the human cortex receives interneuron contributions from cortical proliferative zones beyond the GE-only mouse model.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Furthermore, there is evidence for species differences in the developmental origin of cortical GABAergic interneurons.
findings.effect_size: Interneuron module enriched in human cortical VZ (in addition to GE)
findings.evidence: Microarray of laser-microdissected fetal brain; module eigengene plots show GABAergic interneuron modules expressed in cortical VZ as well as MGE/CGE/LGE.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Extended Data Figure 6 Gene sets corresponding to GABAergic interneurons and proliferating layers also are highly expressed in the ganglionic eminences To examine the relationship between genes enriched in the cortical VZ, including gene modules associated with GABAergic interneurons and mitotically

findings.claim: Human cortex maintains the cross-species pattern in which CGE-derived (ADARB2-branch) inhibitory neurons show greater diversity in superficial layers (L1–L3) and MGE-derived (LHX6-branch) interneurons show greater diversity in deeper layers (L4–L6) — confirming that the laminar bias of CGE/MGE-derived diversity is conserved between mouse and human cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Consistent with mouse, the ADARB2 branch showed more diversity in L1-3 versus L4-6, and the opposite was true for the LHX6 branch ( Fig. 3 , Extended Data Fig. 10 ).
findings.effect_size: ADARB2-branch diversity peaks in L1-L3; LHX6-branch diversity peaks in L4-L6 (conserved mouse↔human)
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq of human MTG with layer-resolved nuclei dissection; cluster diversity (number of types) per layer compared to mouse cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Consistent with mouse, the ADARB2 branch showed more diversity in L1-3 versus L4-6, and the opposite was true for the LHX6 branch ( Fig. 3 , Extended Data Fig. 10 ).
findings.study_system: human MTG (snRNA-seq) vs. mouse cortex literature

findings.claim: Human cortical 'rosehip' cells, originally described as a primate-specific layer-1 GABAergic interneuron subtype, correspond transcriptomically to the Inh L1-4 LAMP5 LCP2 type — although they are present across multiple cortical layers despite their initial L1 description, refining their developmental and morphological classification within the CGE-derived LAMP5/PAX6 subclass.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Inh L1-4 LAMP5 LCP2 matched rosehip cells ( Extended Data Fig. 5d ), discovered in L1 14 but present in all cortical layers.
findings.effect_size: 1 transcriptomic type (Inh L1-4 LAMP5 LCP2) corresponds to rosehip cells; spans L1-L4
findings.evidence: Cross-mapping of published rosehip-cell single-cell RNA-seq (Boldog 2018) to human MTG taxonomy; layer-resolved snRNA-seq.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Inh L1-4 LAMP5 LCP2 matched rosehip cells ( Extended Data Fig. 5d ), discovered in L1 14 but present in all cortical layers.
findings.study_system: human MTG (snRNA-seq); cross-reference to Boldog 2018 rosehip discovery

findings.claim: A hippocampal-formation-specific Vip Cbln4 HPF supertype (clusters #54-55, marked by Qrfpr expression also seen in CA3) is identified as anatomically and molecularly distinct from neocortical Vip cells, demonstrating regional specialization within the CGE-derived VIP lineage that distinguishes hippocampus from isocortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Clusters #54–55 in supertype Vip Cbln4 HPF are marked by Qrfpr , also expressed in CA3 ( Fig. 2E ).
findings.effect_size: 1 hippocampus-specific Vip supertype (Vip Cbln4 HPF, 2 clusters)
findings.evidence: 1.3M-cell scRNA-seq taxonomy of mouse cortex+HPF; identification of HPF-restricted Vip supertype.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Clusters #54–55 in supertype Vip Cbln4 HPF are marked by Qrfpr , also expressed in CA3 ( Fig. 2E ).

findings.claim: Within the cortical VIP+ interneuron population, a major non-calretinin-coexpressing subtype is the rapid-adapting / fast-adapting / IS2 cell, which targets other interneurons (interneuron-selective IS2 functional class) and is electrophysiologically distinct from the VIP+/CR+ bipolar IS3-type population.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These cells have been named rapid-adapting ( Butt et al., 2005 ), fast-adapting ( Lee et al., 2010 ; Miyoshi et al., 2010 ), and IS2 ( Porter et al., 1998 ) and seem to be the major VIP subtype that does not coexpress CR.
findings.effect_size: VIP IS2 / rapid-adapting subtype is the major VIP+/CR- type
findings.evidence: Cross-referenced electrophysiological characterization studies (Butt 2005; Lee 2010; Miyoshi 2010; Porter 1998) plus VIP/CR co-expression analysis.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These cells have been named rapid-adapting ( Butt et al., 2005 ), fast-adapting ( Lee et al., 2010 ; Miyoshi et al., 2010 ), and IS2 ( Porter et al., 1998 ) and seem to be the major VIP subtype that does not coexpress CR.

findings.claim: Within the medial ganglionic eminence (MGE), dorsal vs. ventral progenitor regions bias toward different MGE-derived interneuron subtypes — somatostatin-expressing interneurons preferentially originate from dorsal MGE while parvalbumin-expressing interneurons preferentially originate from ventral MGE.
findings.claim_source_sentence: It has also been reported that different regions of the MGE give rise to different subtypes, with progenitors giving rise to SST and PV being biased to dorsal versus ventral MGE, respectively ( Flames et al., 2007 ; Fogarty et al., 2007 ; Wonders et al., 2008 ).
findings.effect_size: Dorsal MGE → SST-biased; ventral MGE → PV-biased
findings.evidence: Compendium of fate-mapping/transplantation studies (Flames 2007; Fogarty 2007; Wonders 2008) probing dorsal/ventral MGE-restricted progenitor pools.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: It has also been reported that different regions of the MGE give rise to different subtypes, with progenitors giving rise to SST and PV being biased to dorsal versus ventral MGE, respectively ( Flames et al., 2007 ; Fogarty et al., 2007 ; Wonders et al., 2008 ).

findings.claim: VIP-expressing interneurons constitute approximately 15% of all cortical GABAergic interneurons and are predominantly located in superficial cortical layers, defining VIP as one of three major cardinal cortical interneuron classes alongside PV (~40%) and SST (~30%).
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP demarcates the third major class of interneurons, comprising ~15% of all interneurons 81 , 82 and are mostly located in the superficial layers of cortex.
findings.effect_size: ~15% of cortical GABAergic interneurons are VIP+; predominantly L2/3 (superficial)
findings.evidence: Review synthesis of immunohistochemistry/genetic-fate-mapping data; cited primary studies estimate population fractions in mouse cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: VIP demarcates the third major class of interneurons, comprising ~15% of all interneurons 81 , 82 and are mostly located in the superficial layers of cortex.

figure_data.metric: % of cortical interneurons that are VIP+
figure_data.context: predominantly superficial layers (L2/3)

findings.claim: Mayer et al. (2018) profiled 5,622 MGE + 7,401 CGE + 8,543 LGE single cells via Drop-seq with average 1,626 UMI/cell, plus 400 Dlx6a-Cre;RCE-negative GE cells via Smart-seq2 (deeper sequencing) for mitotic-progenitor enrichment, and additional 10x Genomics datasets from Lhx6-GFP-sorted MGE/CGE for cross-validation — establishing a multi-platform single-cell developmental atlas of mouse ganglionic eminences.
findings.claim_source_sentence: After cell dissociation, we utilized Drop-seq 9 to sequence the transcriptomes of 5,622 single cells from the MGE, 7,401 from the CGE, and 8,543 from the LGE, from replicate experiments, observing on average 1626 UMI/cell.
findings.effect_size: 21,566+ Drop-seq GE cells + 400 Smart-seq2 + 10x validation
findings.evidence: Drop-seq, Smart-seq2 (custom), and 10x Genomics scRNA-seq across mouse E13.5/E14.5 ganglionic eminences and FACS-sorted Lhx6+/Lhx6- populations.

findings.claim: A second postmitotic GE branch (precursor state 2) expresses canonical projection-neuron marker genes and is enriched in LGE-derived cells, distinguishing the LGE-derived projection neuron lineage (e.g., medium spiny neuron precursors) from interneuron precursor states (state 1) that express Arx and Maf — formalizing the dichotomy between LGE-derived projection neurons and CGE/MGE-derived cortical interneurons.

findings.claim: Of 133 transcriptomic cortical cell types defined by Tasic et al. (2018), nearly all GABAergic interneuron types are shared between visual cortex (VISp) and anterior lateral motor cortex (ALM); only Sst-Tac1-Tacr3 and Pvalb-Reln-Itm2a are VISp-specific — establishing that GABAergic interneuron diversity is broadly area-conserved across functionally distinct mouse cortical regions, in stark contrast to glutamatergic types which are largely area-specific.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Almost all GABAergic types contain cells from both ALM and VISp (Figs. 1c , 2a ) with the exception of Sst-Tac1-Tacr3 and Pvalb-Reln-Itm2a types, which are VISp-specific.
findings.effect_size: ≥98% of GABAergic types shared VISp/ALM (only 2 area-specific types out of ~60 GABAergic)
findings.evidence: 23,822 single cells from VISp+ALM mouse cortex; 133 transcriptomic types; cell-area composition per type quantified.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Almost all GABAergic types contain cells from both ALM and VISp (Figs. 1c , 2a ) with the exception of Sst-Tac1-Tacr3 and Pvalb-Reln-Itm2a types, which are VISp-specific.

figure_data.metric: Total transcriptomic cell types in mouse VISp+ALM
Types shared between VISp and ALM
